bio.philosophy: 「フレンチを日常使いに」をコンセプトに、フレンチシェフ考案の本格小皿料理や串焼きをリーズナブルに提供。コース不要でアラカルト注文可能。
roles: 運営会社: 有限会社らくちん
総料理長: 吉丸清孝

anecdotes.text: 姫路店限定の「朝フレンチマン」（平日10:00–13:00、¥770）はホットサンド＋サラダ＋ドリンク10種類から選択。施設の開店時間に合わせた姫路限定モーニングとして話題になった。
新大阪の旗艦店（7坪・16席）は月商650万円を達成。大阪駅前第3ビル店（10坪・19席）は月商1,100万円（坪商100万円超）を記録し、渋谷サクラステージ店では2024年11月に月商1,300万円を突破した。

gf_cross_contamination.text: パスタ（リングイネ）、ホットサンド（朝メニュー）など小麦を含む料理が多数あり、調理場での交差汚染リスクが高いと推定される。公式情報なし。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: フレンチベースのため醤油使用は限定的と思われるが、和風アレンジ料理（串焼き等）でのたれ・醤油使用の有無は未確認。

vegan_cross_contact.text: フォアグラ・バター・生クリームなど動物性食材がメニューの中核を担っており、ヴィーガン対応の区別は困難と推定。公式のアレルギー対応情報なし。

staff_allergy_handling.text: アレルギー対応に関する公式情報・スタッフ訓練の案内は取材記事・公式サイト・食べログのいずれにも記載がない。来店前に電話（079-280-4555）での確認を推奨。

positives.text: 野菜を活かした小皿料理が複数あり、野菜中心の食事を好む方には選択肢が存在する。

caveats: グルテンフリー・ヴィーガン対応に関する公式情報は一切確認できなかった。フレンチ料理の性質上、小麦・乳製品・動物性食材が広く使われる。セリアック病・重篤なアレルギーのある方は来店前に必ず電話確認を。

name_ja: おだしとワインとお料理と。motto
name_en: Odashi to Wine to Oryouri to. Motto
cuisine: Dashi-based Japanese dining bar, wine bar
address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市駅前町350 協同ビル 1F
geocode_note: GSI geocode of 兵庫県姫路市駅前町３５０番地 → [134.692215, 34.829494]. Within target box (lat 34.823–34.843, lng 134.679–134.705).
neighborhood: 駅前町 (Ekimae-cho), Miyuki-dori arcade, ~140m from JR Himeji Station north exit / ~5 min walk from Sanyo Himeji Station
seats_text: 34 seats total: 10 counter seats, 24 table seats; semi-private rooms for 4–8 people; max 36 for events
cultural_comfort_note: A corporate-backed Himeji dining bar near the station with menu cards and a relaxed casual atmosphere; minimal English expected but staff are accustomed to tourists visiting the area.
vegan_detail: Dashi is the core identity of the restaurant — the shrimp-broth ramen (ebisoba), bonito-broth oden (洋風おでん), chashu pork ramen toppings, cured ham plates, and Kobe/Tajima beef dishes all make vegan eating effectively impossible here. No plant-based or vegan options are documented on any source.
gf_detail: No dedicated gluten-free menu exists. The signature ebisoba comes in shoyu (soy sauce) and miso versions — both almost certainly contain wheat. Wonton wrappers and shrimp fritters are fried toppings with wheat. The oden uses bonito broth but wheat-containing soy sauce is likely in the base. The obanzai small plates (egg dashi omelette, roasted beef, vegetable sides) may have lower wheat exposure but no kitchen gluten protocols are documented. Cross-contamination risk is unquantified. Celiac diners should contact the restaurant directly before visiting.

bio.background: Opened March 22, 2024 by Maneki Foods (まねき食品株式会社), a Himeji institution founded in 1888 that is credited with inventing ekisoba (Japan's original station noodles served on the Himeji station platform) and an early Makunouchi bento. This restaurant represents a new dining concept from the company — its first attempt at a wine-forward casual dining bar beyond its railway food heritage.
bio.specialty: Ebisoba — shrimp ramen in three broth styles (shoyu, shio, miso) using broth extracted from whole shrimp shells, topped with fried wonton, chashu, and a spiced shrimp fritter
bio.philosophy: Japanese hospitality expressed through dashi: the kitchen frames every dish around carefully prepared broth, pairing it with a curated selection of domestic natural wines chosen with input from Tatsumi Takuo, an actor and chairman of the Japan Wine Lovers Association. The concept was born from conversations between Maneki Foods president Takeda Noriotaka and Tatsumi — a desire to create a space where younger diners could enjoy wine without formality.

anecdotes.text: The restaurant occupies the former site of a jewelry store on Miyuki-dori and was designed in terracotta and earthy brown tones to evoke the orange color of the signature shrimp broth.
Maneki Foods, the operator, has been making ekisoba at Himeji Station since 1949 and is widely credited as the originator of the station-noodle format now found at railways across Japan.
The wine list was personally curated by actor and wine advocate Tatsumi Takuo, featuring eight domestic natural wines with seasonal rotation and rare bottles offered at accessible prices.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No kitchen gluten protocols or dedicated preparation areas are documented in any source. The menu includes fried items (shrimp fritters, fried wontons) alongside non-fried dishes, suggesting shared frying or preparation areas are likely.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Shoyu (soy sauce) versions of the ebisoba and likely the oden broth base will contain wheat. Standard Japanese soy sauce (shoyu) contains wheat unless specifically labeled tamari or GF.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Shrimp broth, bonito dashi, chashu pork, ham, and beef dishes are core to the menu; plant-based cross-contact with animal products is unavoidable in this kitchen.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy handling procedures, allergen menus, or staff training documentation appear on any source. Phone reservations are accepted, which provides an opportunity to discuss dietary needs before visiting: 079-263-7388.

positives.text: The restaurant is entirely non-smoking, which reduces one environmental irritant for sensitive diners.

caveats: Hours cross-checked across Tabelog, official site, and Hotpepper — minor discrepancies in dinner start time (17:00 vs 17:30 on some days); Mon/Wed/Thu evening start listed as 17:00 per official site and Hotpepper, 17:30 per Tabelog for Fri only. Hours may have changed since opening in March 2024; verify before visiting. GF and vegan assessment is conservative: no formal allergy documentation exists from any source, and the dashi-centric concept makes both diets very difficult. Independent=false: operated by Maneki Foods, a publicly listed Himeji corporation.

address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市本町68-170 大手前第1ビル 1F
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 姫路市本町68番地 returned (34.835873, 134.687363). Estimated ~530 m from target center (34.8331, 134.6921) — well within 1.15 km radius.
neighborhood: 本町・大手前第1ビル（大手前通り）
seats_text: 16席（カウンター4席、テーブル12席）— Tabelog listing
cultural_comfort_note: Small 16-seat counter-and-table shop run by a local family; no evidence of English menus or staff — best suited to diners comfortable ordering in Japanese.
vegan_detail: The entire menu revolves around meat — the signature Hamburg steak and beef cutlet are the house pillars, with demi-glace sauce (meat-based), egg-wrapped omelette rice, and tonkatsu. No plant-based or vegan dishes identified on any source. Hidden animal products are pervasive: demi-glace sauce is made with bone stock, egg is used throughout, butter appears in bread service.
gf_detail: Yoshoku is structurally wheat-heavy: the signature demi-glace sauce is flour-thickened, Hamburg steak typically contains breadcrumbs, tonkatsu is panko-breaded, omelette rice uses soy sauce containing wheat. No gluten-free menu, allergen chart, or dedicated preparation protocol is mentioned on any source — Tabelog, the official site, the tourism board listing, or blog coverage. Cross-contamination risk is high in a 16-seat kitchen with no documented GF workflow. Not suitable for celiac or gluten-sensitive diners.

bio.background: The Tenpei name traces back to a post-war izakaya opened by Chef Seki's grandparents. That establishment evolved into Kappo Tenpei before Chef Seki transformed it into Grill TENPEI in 2010, anchoring the family culinary lineage in Western-style cooking.
bio.specialty: Himeji Beef Juice Hamburg — a hand-kneaded patty paired with the house three-day demi-glace sauce, designed to release a rush of juices on the first cut.
bio.philosophy: Chef Seki describes the restaurant as the place he never had growing up in a restaurant family: a spot where three generations can sit down together over a proper meal. The name 'Tenpei' also reflects his personal ethos — meeting the world with flat, unresisting openness and gratitude for nature's bounty. He sources pesticide-free local vegetables, grows some produce himself, and uses Shiga Koshihikari rice.

anecdotes.text: The restaurant bills itself as the world's closest Western restaurant to Himeji Castle — roughly 300 steps from the keep — a distinction that has become a defining part of its identity.
Since 2018, Chef Seki runs a children's food program (子ども食堂) out of the restaurant, accepting donations so local children can share a proper meal and company.
The three-day demi-glace sauce — the foundation of nearly every dish — is made entirely in-house and has been described by a diner as 'rich and deep, worth every minute of the wait.'

gf_cross_contamination.text: No dedicated GF fryer or preparation area is documented. The kitchen produces breaded tonkatsu (panko) and flour-thickened demi-glace simultaneously — cross-contamination is an inherent and unaddressed risk.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard Japanese soy sauce (wheat-containing) is expected in yoshoku cooking, including omelette rice and sauce bases. No tamari or wheat-free soy sauce alternative is mentioned on any source.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Meat stock, butter, egg, and dairy appear across the entire menu. No plant-based station or vegan workflow is documented.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergen menu, staff allergy training, or dietary accommodation protocol is mentioned on Tabelog, the official website, the tourism board listing, or any blog review. Diners with serious allergies should call ahead: 079-280-4697.

positives.text: Chef Seki emphasises minimal use of chemical seasonings and additives, and sources pesticide-free local vegetables — a cleaner ingredient baseline than many casual yoshoku restaurants.

caveats: Hours conflict between sources: Tabelog lists dinner every Mon/Wed-Sun (17:00-21:00); the Himeji tourism board states weekday lunch only. Tabelog is treated as primary. Payment conflict: Tabelog says cash only; Retty mentions cards/PayPay — confirm before visiting. Seat count varies slightly across sources (16 vs 18); Tabelog figure used.

address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市本町68 大手前通り
geocode_note: GSI AddressSearch returned 134.687363, 34.835873 for 兵庫県姫路市本町６８番地. Approximately 529 m from target center (34.8331, 134.6921), well within 1.15 km radius.
neighborhood: 本町 / 大手前通り
cuisine: 創作料理・居酒屋・ビストロ
vegan_detail: The menu is built around meat and seafood — wagyu carpaccio, salmon katsu, sea bream meunière, roast pork, spiced chicken wings, and karaage. No plant-based or vegan alternatives appear on any published menu or course listing. Vegans should not expect accommodation here.
gf_detail: The kitchen relies heavily on wheat: meunière uses wheat flour coating, katsu (salmon rare cutlet) is breaded, karaage and chicken wings are typically soy-sauce marinated and wheat-floured, and the menu includes pizza. Standard Japanese soy sauce (含む小麦) is almost certainly used throughout. No gluten-free options, dedicated fryer, or allergen accommodations are mentioned on any platform. This is an unsafe environment for celiac diners without direct staff consultation — and even then, cross-contamination risk is high given the menu style.
seats_text: 28 seats: 4 counter seats and 24 table seats (Tabelog); HotPepper lists 26 total. Entirely non-smoking.
cultural_comfort_note: A small independent spot on the tourist-friendly Ōtemae-dōri corridor between Himeji Station and the castle; basic Japanese or pointing at menus should work, but no English menu or staff language ability has been documented.

bio.background: Opened in November 2025 on Ōtemae-dōri, the main boulevard linking Himeji Station to Himeji Castle. The name appears to derive from French — the Instagram handle is @faveur_himeji — suggesting a French-bistro inspiration layered onto a Japanese izakaya format.
bio.specialty: Salmon rare cutlet (サーモンのレアカツ) and wagyu seared carpaccio are highlighted signature dishes across multiple platforms; sea bream meunière is consistently praised in early reviews.
bio.philosophy: The concept blends daytime café dining with evening izakaya service, leaning on French bistro technique applied to fresh seafood and selected meats.

gf_cross_contamination.text: The kitchen prepares breaded katsu, meunière (wheat-flour coated), karaage, and pizza — all high-gluten preparations. No information on fryer separation or allergen protocols has been published anywhere.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard wheat-containing soy sauce is almost certainly used in marinades and seasoning throughout the menu; no tamari or gluten-free soy sauce alternative is mentioned on any platform.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The entire menu is built on meat and seafood with no plant-based dishes; animal-derived ingredients including dashi, meat stocks, and dairy (Basque cheesecake) are core to the cooking.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergen menu, allergy-handling policy, or staff dietary-accommodation language appears on Tabelog, HotPepper, Gurunavi, or Hitosara. Diners with serious allergies should call ahead: 050-8885-9357.

caveats: Opened November 2025; still relatively new with limited review history. Hours confirmed across multiple platforms but the Hitosara listing notes hours may differ from published — confirm before visiting. Seat count varies slightly between sources (26 vs 28); 28 is from Tabelog. No official standalone website found; Instagram (@faveur_himeji) is the closest equivalent. No dietary safety information exists for this venue.

name_ja: 手打蕎麦処 やえもん
address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市紺屋町77 KJビル1F
geocode_note: GSI address-search on 兵庫県姫路市紺屋町77 returned [134.693542, 34.830376]. Approximately 330m from target center — well within the 1.15km radius.
neighborhood: 山陽姫路駅周辺（紺屋町・おみぞ筋商店街）
seats_text: カウンター5席、テーブル2卓8席、座敷2卓12席（計25席）。大きな窓から蕎麦打ちの様子が見える。
hours_raw: 火〜日・祝 11:00〜20:30（LO 20:00）、月曜定休。複数情報源で閉店時間に差異あり（20:30〜22:00）。最新はInstagram @yaemon0301 で確認推奨。
cultural_comfort_note: おみぞ筋商店街の地元向け蕎麦屋。英語メニューや英語対応スタッフの情報はなく、日本語のみの対応と思われる。
gf_detail: 手打ちそばは buckwheat（蕎麦粉）と小麦粉を混合するのが一般的で、グルテンフリー対応の言及はなし。つゆ（返し）には小麦を含む醤油を使用、天ぷらには小麦粉の衣を使用。出汁はかつおベースとみられる（小麦不含の可能性はあるが未確認）。セリアック/小麦アレルギーには非対応と判断。
vegan_detail: 出汁はかつお節ベースと推定。メニューに天ぷら・天丼・カツ丼・エビ・ホタテなど動物性食材が多数。ヴィーガン対応の記載はなし。

bio.background: 2017年3月1日、姫路のおみぞ筋商店街に開店。オーナーは出石（いずし）の蕎麦店で修業を積んだ経歴を持ち、約20年の蕎麦打ち経験を経て独立した。カフェ跡地を改装し、「サラリーマンにも家族にも愛される地元の店になりたい」との思いで開業した。
bio.specialty: 手打ち蕎麦・サクサク天ぷら・自家製わらび餅
bio.philosophy: 麺と出汁にこだわった本格的な蕎麦。毎日手打ちし、大きな窓越しに蕎麦打ちの姿を通りから見せることで、丁寧な仕事を伝える。

anecdotes.text: 開店以来ランチタイムは満席になるほどの人気。8年経った2025年現在も昼時は行列ができる
開業時からの人気デザート「自家製わらび餅」は食事後に提供され、多くのリピーターに愛されている

gf_cross_contamination.text: グルテン交差汚染に関する情報は一切公表されていない。手打ちそばに小麦粉が含まれ、天ぷら（小麦衣）も同一厨房で調理されるため、セリアック患者への対応は期待できない。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: そばつゆの返しには一般的に小麦を含む醤油が使用されると推定されるが、店舗から明示的な確認情報はなし。

vegan_cross_contact.text: 出汁はかつお節ベースが標準的。ヴィーガン向け出汁への切り替えや分離調理に関する情報はなし。

staff_allergy_handling.text: アレルギー対応・グルテンフリー対応に関するスタッフ訓練や情報開示は確認できていない。事前に電話（079-281-9555）で確認を推奨。

caveats: 営業時間は情報源により11:00-20:30〜22:00まで差異あり。最新情報はInstagram @yaemon0301 または電話で要確認。現金のみとの情報があるが、一部ソースではPayPay可との記載もあり、訪問前に確認推奨。席数は情報源により25〜40席と差異あり。

name_ja: クラフトジンとおでん専門店 maruya
name_en: Craft Gin to Oden Senmonten Maruya
address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市立町55-2
geocode_note: GSI AddressSearch on 兵庫県姫路市立町55-2 returned coordinates [134.689758, 34.83176], well within the 1.15km target box.
neighborhood: 立町 (Tatemachi), 339m from Sanyo Himeji Station
seats_text: 7 counter seats on the ground floor; second floor is private rental only (groups of 3+)
closed_days: 月曜定休（月に1度の火曜も休業あり）
cultural_comfort_note: A small, intimate 7-seat counter bar run by a solo owner; Japanese is the working language and the setting is local izakaya-style, though the casual counter atmosphere is approachable for non-Japanese speakers willing to point and gesture.
vegan_detail: Oden dashi is made from Rausu kombu (kelp), bonito flakes, and tuna flakes — not vegan. The ginger soy sauce served alongside is likely wheat-containing standard shoyu. Vegetable oden pieces (daikon, konnyaku, tofu) may exist but are cooked in the same fish-based broth. No vegan accommodation is mentioned anywhere; ask staff before ordering.
gf_detail: No gluten-free claims made. Oden is served with ginger soy sauce (shoga joyu), which is standard wheat-containing shoyu — a direct gluten source for celiacs. Dashi uses bonito and tuna flakes (inherently GF) but the shared broth pot and condiments present cross-contamination risk. No dedicated fryer noted (oden is simmered, not fried). Celiac diners should call ahead; safe eating here is not confirmed.

bio.background: Kotani spent 15 years as a bartender at an English pub in Himeji before opening maruya in October 2023, fulfilling a long-held dream to combine his two passions.
bio.specialty: Craft gin paired with Himeji-style oden simmered in a dashi of Rausu kombu, bonito, and tuna — served with ginger soy sauce in the local Himeji tradition.
bio.philosophy: Kotani draws a philosophical parallel between oden and craft gin: 'Oden varies by region, household, and broth; craft gin creates infinite flavors through botanicals. Both are undefined and free — they share common ground.' He aims for Japan's finest dashi-driven oden.

anecdotes.text: A regular at the counter mistakenly called Kotani by the wrong surname — triggering collective laughter among the other guests, a moment that captures the relaxed, locals-bar warmth of the space.
Kotani stocks around 45 varieties of domestic craft gin and offers an all-you-can-drink plan featuring 40 of them for ¥4,000 — an unusual proposition in a city better known for sake.

gf_cross_contamination.text: The shared oden pot simmers all ingredients together; no separate preparation for allergen-free orders is mentioned.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Oden is served with ginger soy sauce (shoga joyu), which is standard wheat-containing shoyu and a direct gluten source. No tamari or GF soy sauce alternative is mentioned.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Dashi broth is made with Rausu kombu, bonito flakes, and tuna flakes. All oden items, including vegetables, are cooked in this fish-based broth — unavoidable cross-contact for vegans.

positives.text: Oden is a simmered dish with no deep-frying involved, eliminating fryer cross-contamination as a concern.

caveats: Hours are irregular: the shop occasionally closes on a Tuesday once per month in addition to the regular Monday closure — confirm before visiting. Reservation must be made at least 2 days in advance outside business hours. Cash only. The second floor is private rental only and not available for walk-in seating.

address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市本町68 姫小路2F
geocode_note: GSI matched 兵庫県姫路市本町６８番地; coordinates [134.687363, 34.835873] are within the target box and approx 520m from the Himeji Castle–Station midpoint.
neighborhood: 本町 (大手前通り)
vegan_detail: All lunch sets and muffins are 100% vegan; no animal-derived ingredients used in any menu item. Sweets use rice flour and okara (soy pulp) in place of wheat flour and eggs; almond powder is deliberately excluded to accommodate nut allergies. Soy milk is used in lattes. The kitchen can prepare dishes gokunin-nuki (五葷抜き — without garlic, onions, chives, pickled rakkyo, and shallots) on request, making items suitable for Buddhist-style vegan diners as well. No hidden dashi, katsuo, or bonito noted in any source.
gf_detail: Multiple sources note that most items are gluten-free or GF-adaptable: rice-flour-based sweets, multigrain rice sets, and vegan taco rice are cited as GF-friendly. However, no dedicated GF certification exists and no source explicitly confirms wheat-free soy sauce or a dedicated GF preparation zone. Cross-contamination protocols are not documented anywhere. The café is a tiny 9-seat solo-operated kitchen, making strict separation difficult to verify. Celiacs should contact the café directly before visiting — phone 090-9087-0734 or Instagram @cafe_kotonaki.
closed_days: 水曜日（祝日の場合は営業）
seats_text: カウンター5席、2人テーブル×2卓（計9席）
cultural_comfort_note: The owner actively engages international visitors; an English menu is available and the café sits steps from Himeji Castle, welcoming foreign tourists by design.
caveats: Hours on Vegewel list 11:00–19:00, while Tabelog and multiple blogs confirm 11:00–20:00 (Mon/Tue/Thu) and 11:00–22:00 (Fri–Sun); the later hours are from the primary Tabelog listing and are used here. Cash-only status was reported at launch (himeji-spice.net) but Tabelog notes card and QR payment were being added; confirm on arrival. GF information is limited — no source documents dedicated GF preparation or wheat-free soy sauce.

bio.background: Iwasaki Kano honed her plant-based cooking skills at TEEDA CAFE (ティーダカフェ), a cave-themed café near Eiga-bo Station in Himeji, where she handled both savory cooking and pastry. On July 13, 2025, she opened コトナキ solo on the second floor of the 姫小路 building — a 70-year-old renovated wooden structure steps from Himeji Castle on Otemae-dori.
bio.specialty: Vegan taco rice (¥1,500) — spiced mung beans and soy protein over multigrain brown rice with housemade salsa; and daily-rotation vegan muffins made with rice flour and okara.
bio.philosophy: The name derives from the Japanese phrase 事なきを得る (kotonaki wo eru) — 'to come through without incident, to be safe.' Iwasaki's vision is a calm refuge where people of any background — vegan, allergy-affected, health-conscious, or simply curious — can settle in and breathe. She runs the space alone, savoring conversations with travelers from around the world.

anecdotes.text: Iwasaki deliberately excludes almond powder from her baked goods so that guests with nut allergies can eat freely alongside vegan and allergy-conscious diners.
The glassware used for the gateau au chocolat is made by local Himeji artist Toki Kurogawa, giving each dessert a handcrafted, gallery-like presentation.
At opening, the café forged a cross-venue pairing partnership with nearby KUUHAKU COFFEE HIMEJI, so guests can bring drinks between the two spots.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No dedicated GF preparation zone or cross-contamination protocols have been documented in any published source. The kitchen is a compact solo-operated space; separation cannot be assumed.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No source confirms whether wheat-free tamari or GF soy sauce is used. Standard Japanese soy sauce contains wheat; celiacs should ask explicitly before ordering.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All menu items are stated to be 100% plant-based; no animal products are used in the kitchen, so vegan cross-contact risk appears negligible based on the café's stated concept.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The owner is allergy-aware and deliberately excludes almond powder from sweets; she also accommodates gokunin-nuki (five-pungent-free) requests. However, formal allergen-handling procedures are not documented.

positives.text: Entire menu is 100% plant-based — no fish, meat, dairy, or eggs used anywhere in the kitchen, eliminating the main vegan cross-contact vectors.
Sweets are made with rice flour and okara instead of wheat flour, meaning several dessert items are naturally gluten-free in their base formulation.
English menu available; owner is communicative with foreign guests, making dietary questions easier to raise.

address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市南町12 北道ビル１Ｆ
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 兵庫県姫路市南町１２番地; approximately 0.5km from target center, well within 1.15km radius.
neighborhood: 山陽姫路駅周辺（駅から徒歩1〜3分）
seats_text: 12 counter seats and 20 tatami-style seats (five tables of four)
cultural_comfort_note: Small local restaurant run by a couple with no English menu or signage evident; basic Japanese helpful for ordering.
vegan_detail: The menu is centered on deep-fried skewers of meat, seafood, and vegetables, all cooked in the same fryer with panko breadcrumbs. No vegan or vegetarian options are listed or suggested anywhere on the menu or official site. Fish carpaccio appears in every course. Hidden dashi in sauces is also probable.
gf_detail: Kushiage is by definition coated in wheat-based panko breadcrumbs and deep-fried; gluten is the core technique. Standard dipping sauces and marinades will contain wheat-based soy sauce. No gluten-free accommodations, dedicated fryer, or allergen information is mentioned on the official website, Tabelog, or any review source. Not safe for celiac or serious gluten intolerance.

bio.background: A long-established hidden-gem kushiage specialist in central Himeji, located on the first floor of a small building a short walk from Sanyo-Himeji Station. The restaurant is run by a couple and has been operating for well over a decade, earning loyal regulars who return year after year. It describes itself as a pioneer of creative kushiage (創作串揚げ) in the area.
bio.specialty: Seasonal kushiage skewers — meat, seafood, and vegetables coated in light panko and deep-fried, served in courses of 10–20 pieces. The menu rotates quarterly to follow the seasons.
bio.philosophy: The kitchen emphasizes seasonal ingredients and dishes that can only be found here, pairing the skewers with wines, plum liqueurs, and shochu chosen by the proprietor.

anecdotes.text: One long-time guest wrote that they first visited just before starting their own business, meaning they have been coming for over a decade — a testament to the restaurant's consistency and warmth.
Reviewers describe the proprietors as a 'pleasant couple' who create a relaxed atmosphere where guests can truly unwind.

gf_cross_contamination.text: All kushiage items are coated in panko (wheat breadcrumbs) and fried in a shared fryer. Cross-contamination with wheat is inherent and unavoidable across the entire menu.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No soy sauce specifications are listed. Standard Japanese soy sauce (which contains wheat) is virtually certain to be used in marinades and dipping sauces; no tamari or gluten-free soy sauce is mentioned.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All items are cooked in the same fryer as meat and seafood. No separation of animal products from vegetables is indicated.

caveats: Payment: the official site lists cash, PayPay, d払い, Merpay, and LINE Pay only — no credit cards. Tabelog may incorrectly show cards accepted; trust the official site. Hours: Sunday/holiday last order is 20:30 (close 21:00), weekday/Saturday last order is 21:30 (close 22:00). Seating: Tabelog lists 12 seats but official site says 32 (12 counter + 20 tatami); the larger figure from the official site is used.

vegan_detail: Fully plant-based — no meat, fish, eggs, dairy, or honey used anywhere on the menu. All ingredients are organic, naturally farmed, or low-pesticide vegetables with additive-free seasonings sourced through the on-site natural food shop. Menu rotates monthly with seasonal macrobiotic lunch sets, sweets, and plant-based beverages including dandelion coffee, brown rice coffee, and grain drinks.
gf_detail: No dedicated gluten-free status. The café explicitly states "our foods are not specified as allergy-free" and asks customers with allergies to contact staff in advance. Miso and soy-based proteins appear prominently in the menu; conventional Japanese soy sauce (which contains wheat) is likely used unless additive-free tamari is substituted — this has not been confirmed. Some individual items such as tofu and rice flour desserts could be GF by ingredient, but no dedicated fryer information is available and cross-contamination protocols are not disclosed. Celiac diners should call ahead before visiting.
address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市呉服町18
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 兵庫県姫路市呉服町18 returned [134.693665, 34.830647]. Approximately 306m from target center (34.8331, 134.6921), well within the 1.15km radius. Tabelog independently confirms 377m from Sanyo Himeji Station.
neighborhood: 呉服町 (Gofukucho)
seats_text: Two floors: upstairs has four 2-person tables plus two counter seats; downstairs has one 4-person table and six counter seats, approximately 18 seats total.
hours_raw: Café 11:30–18:30 (last order 17:30); Shop 10:30–18:30. Closed Mondays and the 3rd Sunday of each month. Lunch last order 15:30.
closed_days: 月曜日・第3日曜日 (Mondays and the 3rd Sunday of each month)
cultural_comfort_note: An English menu version is available and the café attracts health-conscious visitors including foreign tourists near Himeji Castle, but staff communication is primarily Japanese.
caveats: One search snippet (from an AI-summarized result) indicated a temporary closure for menu renewal as of December 2025 with a planned spring 2026 reopening. However, the official website shows current fall/winter 2025 cooking class announcements and seasonal menu posts, and Tabelog shows multiple customer reviews from August 2025 with no closure notice. The hours_week array treats the 3rd Sunday as a regular Sunday closure for simplicity; in practice Sunday is open except on the 3rd week of each month. Confirm hours before visiting.

bio.background: The roots of il riccio stretch back to 1972, when the owner's predecessor natural food shop first opened on Miyuki-dori (みゆき通り) in central Himeji — operating for roughly 48 years before relocating. In November 2019, the business moved to Gofukucho and relaunched as 'Plant-Based Café + Natural Food Shop il riccio,' combining the long-running retail side with a new café component. The current owner is a certified macrobiotic cooking instructor who runs regular cooking classes alongside the café.
bio.specialty: Seasonal one-soup-three-sides (一汁三菜) macrobiotic lunch sets built around organic Hyogo rice, handmade miso, and rotating plant-based mains such as soy-meat cutlet or taro gratin; monthly-changing vegan sweets including tofu and rice flour gateau chocolat.
bio.philosophy: The café embeds macrobiotics into every decision: source organic or naturally farmed local vegetables, use additive-free seasonings from the on-site shop, avoid all animal products, and let food support health and beauty from within. The name 'il riccio' — Italian for hedgehog — was chosen because hedgehogs are a European lucky symbol, and the owner hopes that each person who discovers macrobiotics feels that same sense of good fortune. The secondary meaning of 'riccio' (curly hair) also appealed personally to the owner.

anecdotes.text: The name 'il riccio' (Italian for hedgehog) was chosen deliberately: the owner has long loved hedgehogs and discovered they are considered lucky in European culture. The hope was that anyone who encounters macrobiotics through the shop would feel that same lucky sensation. The Italian language was chosen because the owner loves Italy and the sound of its words — and 'riccio' also means curly hair, which matched the owner's own super-curly hair.
The hedgehog theme carries through to the entrance, where a hedgehog figurine greets visitors at the door of the European-style building in Gofukucho.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information is published about cross-contamination controls, shared equipment, or dedicated preparation areas for allergen-free cooking.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Miso (soy-based, handmade) and soy-derived protein products are used extensively. The specific type of soy sauce used — whether wheat-containing shoyu or wheat-free tamari — has not been disclosed in any published source. Macrobiotic philosophy favours traditionally brewed condiments, which typically contain wheat.

vegan_cross_contact.text: No animal products are used anywhere in the café kitchen; the entire menu is 100% plant-based, so vegan cross-contact from cooking is not a concern here.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The café explicitly states: 'Please note that our foods are not specified as allergy-free. Please let our staff know in advance if you have any allergies.' Customers with allergies are asked to contact the café before visiting.

positives.text: Entire menu is 100% plant-based with no meat, fish, eggs, dairy, or honey — eliminating vegan cross-contact risk entirely.
All seasonings and ingredients are sourced from the on-site natural food shop and are certified organic, naturally farmed, or low-pesticide with no synthetic additives.
Some individual items — such as the tofu and rice flour gateau chocolat — are made with inherently gluten-free ingredients, though no GF certification or dedicated preparation claim is made.

address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市紺屋町28
geocode_note: GSI AddressSearch on 兵庫県姫路市紺屋町28 returned coordinates [134.693497, 34.830097], within the target box and approximately 350 m from the center point.
neighborhood: 紺屋町 / おみぞ筋商店街 — 山陽姫路駅徒歩5分
seats_text: 8席カウンターのみ。テイクアウト可。
cultural_comfort_note: Small 8-seat counter in a local shopping arcade; menu and signage appear to be Japanese only, with no evidence of English-language staff or materials.
vegan_detail: The base shaved ice (Shinshu deep-spring water) is vegan. Fruit-syrup items such as the citrus blend appear dairy-free, but several signature items include condensed milk (練乳), whipped cream, or mochi sauce. No dedicated vegan labeling; hidden dashi is not a concern in a kakigori shop, but dairy presence in popular items is real. Confirm individual flavors on the day.
gf_detail: Plain shaved ice with fruit syrup is naturally wheat-free. Kinako (roasted soy) and mochi toppings are glutinous-rice-based and wheat-free in isolation, but cross-contamination in a small kitchen is unknown. No allergen disclosure is published, no certified GF claim exists. Soy sauce is not used in kakigori preparation so wheat-in-shoyu is not a concern here. Guests with celiac disease should confirm specific toppings and kitchen handling before ordering.

bio.background: Opened April 6, 2024, in a renovated former izakaya space (approximately 6 tsubo) in the Omizo-suji shopping arcade, a covered street running north from Himeji Station. The shop went on hiatus and re-opened June 5, 2026.
bio.specialty: Fluffy kakigori shaved from ultra-high-purity Shinshu deep-groundwater ice (drawn from 250 m depth), dressed with unheated, handmade fresh-fruit syrups.
bio.philosophy: Kakigori as a year-round dessert, not a summer-only treat. The proprietor selected the space for its open-ceiling traditional atmosphere and maintains razor-sharp blades to produce ultra-thin ice that melts without causing a head-freeze sensation. Pickled vegetables are served alongside as a palate cleanser, following the salt-with-sweet principle. Future plans include expanding into fruit sandwiches and tea.

anecdotes.text: The proprietor uses Shinshu deep-spring natural water for its high transparency and clean flavor, shaving it paper-thin so guests can taste the syrup without the ice overwhelming it.
Instagram followers receive a ¥100 discount — a small gesture that reflects the shop's community-first approach in the local arcade.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No allergen or cross-contamination information is published by the shop. Kitchen size (approximately 6 tsubo total) means separation may be limited.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Soy sauce is not used in kakigori preparation, so wheat-via-shoyu is not a relevant risk for this format.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Condensed milk and whipped cream are used in several items and prepared in the same small counter kitchen; dairy cross-contact cannot be ruled out for fruit-only orders.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No information on staff allergy-awareness training or handling protocols has been published. The shop has not advertised any allergen accommodation service.

positives.text: Shaved ice with pure fruit syrup (no dairy topping) is a simple, naturally wheat-free preparation that avoids most common gluten sources.
All syrups are prepared without heat (non-heated raw syrup) from fresh fruit, with no mention of added thickeners or wheat-containing stabilizers.

caveats: The shop had a hiatus of unknown duration and re-opened June 5, 2026 per its Instagram bio. Hours and closure days are based on Tabelog data and opening-era articles; confirm current hours before visiting as they may have changed. Payment: QR code (PayPay etc.) accepted; credit cards and e-money are not accepted. Cash is also accepted (not cash-only). No allergen disclosure is published — guests with celiac disease or severe dairy allergy should call ahead (070-8536-2366).

neighborhood: 南駅前町 (Minami-Ekimae-cho)
address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市南駅前町94 山上ビル１F
geocode_note: GSI address-search on 兵庫県姫路市南駅前町９４番地 returned [134.691452, 34.825436]. Distance to target center ~854m, within the 1.15km radius.
seats_text: カウンター席あり、個室あり（7〜10名）、貸切応相談（最大20名）
cultural_comfort_note: A long-established neighborhood izakaya near Himeji Station with no English menu or signage; staff are likely Japanese-only, but the counter atmosphere is welcoming if you can point and gesture.
vegan_detail: This is a seafood and grilled-chicken izakaya. Every section of the menu — sashimi, charcoal-grilled fish, yakitori skewers, and small plates — is built around animal proteins. No plant-based alternatives are listed or implied by any source reviewed.
gf_detail: Standard izakaya kitchen: soy sauce (wheat-containing) is used in virtually all preparations — marinades, tare for yakitori, dipping sauces, and dashi bases. Tempura is on the menu (battered with wheat flour). There is no GF claim anywhere. Plain sashimi (sashimi only, no sauce) is the most plausible lower-risk item, but cross-contamination from the shared charcoal grill and kitchen surfaces cannot be ruled out. Celiacs should not eat here without direct staff communication in Japanese.

bio.background: A charcoal-fire izakaya that has operated near Himeji Station's south exit for roughly 26 years. The restaurant recently underwent a renovation and added lunch service. It is located on the first floor of the Yamakamibe Building, a one- to two-minute walk from the south exit of JR Himeji Station.
bio.specialty: Seasonal seafood sourced from the Harima-nada (Banshu-nada) sea, served as sashimi or over the charcoal grill; yakitori skewers are also a staple.

anecdotes.text: A reviewer described the atmosphere as 'exceptionally calm and relaxing,' noting they felt drawn to return for the comfortable counter seating and the quality of the yellowtail sashimi and one-night-dried grilled flounder.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Shared charcoal grill used for both seafood and chicken; no separation for allergen-sensitive diners mentioned anywhere.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Soy sauce (wheat-containing) is a fundamental ingredient in izakaya cooking — tare for yakitori, marinades, and dipping sauces all apply here. No tamari or wheat-free soy sauce options are indicated.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All menu items involve seafood or chicken; no vegan dishes exist to evaluate for cross-contact.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy-handling policy or allergen menu is noted on any platform reviewed. Inquiries would need to be made in Japanese directly with staff.

positives.text: Plain sashimi (without soy sauce) is the single category most likely to be free of added wheat — the kitchen does receive and serve raw seafood that has not been marinated.

caveats: Hours from multiple sources consistently show Mon-Sat 17:00-23:00, closed Sunday, but one Tabelog extract mentioned "closed Monday and Sunday" while others say "closed Sunday only." The Mon-Sat open / Sunday closed pattern is confirmed by Gurunavi, HotPepper, and the English Tabelog listing and is treated as authoritative. Seat count: Gurunavi states 70, but HotPepper and AutoReserve both say 27; the lower figure (27) is likely post-renovation and is used here. No official website beyond Instagram was found.

address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市西二階町65 扉ビル1F
geocode_note: GSI geocode of 兵庫県姫路市西二階町６５番地 returned lat 34.832382, lng 134.688477 — approximately 340m from target center, well within 1.15km radius.
neighborhood: 西二階町 / 大手前エリア
seats_text: 14席（カウンター中心、座敷あり）
cultural_comfort_note: Intimate counter-only sushi bar run by a husband-and-wife team; interaction with the master is part of the experience, and communication is entirely in Japanese.
vegan_detail: Traditional Edomae sushi counter built around fish and seafood; no vegan options identified. Sushi rice seasoning, dashi-based side dishes, and fish-centric menu make accommodation highly unlikely without advance arrangement.
gf_detail: Standard Japanese soy sauce (wheat-containing) is virtually certain to be used in nigiri marinades and side dishes. Edomae-style dashi is fish-based, not wheat-relevant, but tamari or wheat-free shoyu is not mentioned anywhere. No dedicated GF menu or allergen information available. Guests with celiac disease should call ahead — there is no evidence the kitchen can accommodate wheat avoidance.

bio.background: A long-established sushi counter in the historic Nishinikaichō shopping street near Himeji Castle, said to be patronized by notable figures in the city. The shop occupies the first floor of the Tobira Building and seats just 14 guests.
bio.specialty: Edomae-style nigiri sushi with seasonal seafood — mackerel, uni, matsutake mushroom accompaniments — prepared with refined knife work including the traditional 隠し包丁 (kakushi bōchō) scoring technique.
bio.philosophy: Quiet craftsmanship: the master and his wife offer attentive, unhurried hospitality at the counter, letting seasonal ingredients speak for themselves.

anecdotes.text: Reviewers consistently praise the master and his wife's hospitality: 'The master and his wife's service was excellent — the sushi and small dishes were outstanding.' The counter's intimate scale means every piece is made to order in front of the guest.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information on cross-contamination controls available. A busy counter kitchen serving multiple courses is high-risk for shared surfaces and utensils.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard wheat-containing soy sauce is standard in Edomae sushi preparation (nigiri brushing, marinades). No tamari or wheat-free shoyu noted anywhere in available sources.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Entire menu centres on fish and seafood; vegan cross-contact is unavoidable.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy or dietary accommodation information found on any platform. Guests should call the restaurant directly before visiting.

caveats: No official website found. Chef name not publicly documented. Operating hours and closed days confirmed across Tabelog, Gnavi, and Retty but should be verified by phone before visiting (079-288-9738). Under30 classification is 'no' based on ¥10,000–¥14,999 per-person price point.

name_ja: 四季と創和の酔い処 ひより
name_en: Shiki to Souwa no Yoidokoro Hiyori
neighborhood: 久保町 (Kubo-cho), near Sanyo-Himeji Station
address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市久保町37
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 兵庫県姫路市久保町37: [134.68721, 34.82893]. Approximately 640m from the Himeji Castle–Station midpoint — within the 1.15km target radius.
seats_text: 17 seats total — counter seats and a semi-private room that fits up to 12 for group bookings.
cultural_comfort_note: A quiet, adult-oriented kappo hideaway with counter seating and a semi-private room; no English signage or menu noted, and dietary requests in English would require preparation.
vegan_detail: No vegan options are listed. The kitchen is built around seafood, wagyu beef, and classic Japanese preparations — virtually every dish will include fish-based dashi (鰹・昆布), and the tempura course uses wheat batter. Vegetarians and vegans should call ahead (080-3818-0270), but accommodation is uncertain for a counter-focused omakase.
gf_detail: No gluten-free options are advertised. The flagship course includes fresh-fish tempura (wheat batter), and standard Japanese soy sauce (含む小麦) is almost certainly used throughout. No dedicated fryer information is available. Celiac diners should not assume safety — direct inquiry in Japanese is essential before visiting.

bio.background: Opened on 11 February 2026 in the Kubo-cho neighbourhood just a few minutes' walk from Sanyo-Himeji Station. The owner-chef personally selects seasonal ingredients daily, positioning the restaurant as a sophisticated adult hideaway (大人の隠れ家) in the kappo-izakaya style.
bio.specialty: Seasonal seafood with a focus on Himeji's prized conger eel (鱧/hamo) and same-morning-caught fish served as sashimi, tataki, or tempura; also features Japanese wagyu beef.
bio.philosophy: Local sourcing and seasonality (地産地消・旬の食材) — the menu changes each day according to what the owner's eye finds at market.

anecdotes.text: Early visitors described the interior as 'very clean' and the spot as a 'hidden gem,' with the salt-grilled sweetfish (鮎の塩焼き) drawing particular praise shortly after opening.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Tempura is a core menu item (fresh fish and seasonal vegetable tempura appear in the standard course), meaning wheat flour is actively used in the kitchen. Cross-contamination for celiacs must be assumed.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No tamari or gluten-free soy sauce is mentioned. Standard wheat-containing soy sauce (醤油) is standard in Japanese kappo cooking and almost certainly used across sashimi, grilled, and vinegared preparations.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Fish-based dashi (鰹・昆布) is foundational to Japanese kappo cuisine and will be present in soups, vinegared dishes, and sauces. No plant-based dashi alternative is noted anywhere.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy handling policy is published. The restaurant is a small 17-seat counter operation; direct phone contact (080-3818-0270) before visiting is the only viable path for allergy inquiries.

positives.text: The owner hand-selects ingredients daily at market, suggesting flexibility around what goes into each dish — a potential opening for custom requests if communicated in advance in Japanese.

caveats: Hours from HotPepper show Tue-Fri lunch (11:30-14:00) + dinner, while Tabelog/Hitosara show dinner-only on Mon-Sat. The discrepancy may reflect a phase-in of lunch service after opening; the dinner hours (17:30-22:30) are consistently reported across all sources. Verify current lunch availability directly. Monday hours set to dinner-only per Tabelog. No official standalone website was found — Instagram (@shikitosowanoyoidokoro) is the closest public channel.

cuisine: Traditional Japanese (Harima regional cuisine) — anago (conger eel) rice bowls, anago tempura, soba, seasonal kaiseki-style sets
address_ja: 〒670-0012 兵庫県姫路市本町68 姫路城西御屋敷跡庭園 好古園内
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI AddressSearch on 兵庫県姫路市本町68; result 34.835873,134.687363 is approximately 0.52 km from target center — well within the 1.15 km radius.
neighborhood: 好古園内 / 姫路城西 (Koko-en Garden, west of Himeji Castle)
gf_detail: This restaurant is not safe for celiac diners. Wheat is present throughout the menu: soba noodles (buckwheat-wheat blended in standard Japanese soba), tempura batter (wheat flour), udon noodles, and conventional soy sauce used in braised anago preparations. No gluten-free menu or allergen accommodation is listed on the official site or mentioned in any review. No dedicated fryer information found. Inquiring in advance would be necessary, and the kitchen is a high-cross-contamination environment.
vegan_detail: No vegetarian menu is offered (explicitly stated by the restaurant on GLTJP). The signature dishes — anago (conger eel) don and tempura — are fish-based. Miso soup, dashi in noodle broths, and sauces almost certainly contain katsuobushi (bonito) or niboshi dashi. One visitor reported their vegetarian partner could only find soybeans and noodles. A zenzai (sweet red bean soup) dessert is noted by one reviewer as likely vegan, but confirmation of dashi-free preparation is unavailable. Suitable only for those with very flexible plant-based preferences who are comfortable eating at an omni restaurant with minimal options.
hours_raw: 10:00–16:00 (last order 15:30); meal service 11:00–15:00; seasonal variation (some sources note 9:30–17:30 in Sep–Apr). Closed December 29–30.
closed_days: December 29–30; hours vary by season (shorter in summer, longer in shoulder seasons)
seats_text: Approximately 40 seats, all arranged to overlook the garden pond and Himeji Castle landscape
cultural_comfort_note: A popular tourist destination attracting many international visitors — basic navigation is manageable for non-Japanese speakers, but staff communication beyond standard ordering is Japanese-first; English menus may be available given the tourist context.

bio.background: Kassui-ken sits inside Koko-en (好古園), a 3.5-hectare traditional Japanese garden built in 1992 on the site of the former Nishi-Oyashiki (west lord's residence) adjacent to Himeji Castle. The restaurant was established as part of the garden complex and is operated by the Himeji City Urban Development Promotion Organization (公益社団法人姫路市まちづくり振興機構), a civic body that manages the garden. Its name, 活水軒 (Kassui-ken), evokes the classical ideal of 'living water' — flowing, nourishing, and ever-renewing — a fitting name for a pavilion beside garden ponds.
bio.specialty: Anago (conger eel) dishes — particularly the anago don (braised conger eel over rice) and anago tempura sets — using the prized conger eel of Harima Bay, a longtime Himeji culinary tradition
bio.philosophy: Showcasing the traditional taste of Himeji through seasonal, locally-rooted Harima cuisine in a setting where the garden and castle become part of the meal

anecdotes.text: Visitors frequently arrive only to find the limited-quantity 'Hime Gozen' and 'Kanbei Bento' sets already sold out by midday — a recurring note in reviews urging early arrival.
During peak autumn foliage season the wait can exceed 35 minutes even after reducing the initial queue through a garden walk — a rhythm regulars have learned to work with.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Tempura is a core menu item (anago tempura, vegetable tempura) cooked in shared kitchen oil using wheat-flour batter. No dedicated fryer or allergen separation is documented.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Braised anago (conger eel) dishes rely on conventional Japanese soy sauce (shoyu), which contains wheat. No tamari or GF soy sauce alternative is mentioned anywhere.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Dashi stock (likely katsuobushi/bonito-based) is standard in miso soup, noodle broths, and simmered preparations throughout the menu. No plant-based dashi option is mentioned.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergen menu, allergy chart, or documented staff allergy-handling procedure appears on the official site or in any guest review. The restaurant does not list dietary accommodations as a feature.

positives.text: Non-smoking throughout; Himeji-area Wi-Fi available; credit cards and QR code payments accepted, reducing cash-handling stress for visitors.

caveats: Hours vary seasonally and some sources conflict (10:00–16:00 vs 9:30–17:30 in cooler months). The official site should be checked before visiting. Reservations are currently suspended; walk-in only, with significant waits during spring/autumn peak seasons. Limited-quantity signature sets sell out early. Seat count of ~40 is from a travel blog, not an official source.

name_ja: あなご料理 柊 本店
address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市十二所前町88
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 兵庫県姫路市十二所前町８８番地 returned [134.6866, 34.830769]; approximately 564m from target center — within 1.15km radius.
neighborhood: 十二所前町（山陽姫路駅より徒歩6分、JR姫路駅より徒歩7分）
vegan_detail: This is a fish-specialist restaurant dedicated entirely to conger eel. Every dish centres on anago: sashimi, grilled, steamed, tempura, rice bowls, and kaiseki courses. There are no plant-based mains or sides designed for vegans, and all broths and cooking sauces will contain fish-derived ingredients. Vegans have no practical options here.
gf_detail: Wheat is pervasive throughout this menu. Standard Japanese soy sauce (含む小麦) is used in grilled and simmered preparations; tempura items are battered with wheat flour; dashi is fish-based (anago/konbu) but shoyu marinades add wheat. No gluten-free soy sauce or dedicated fryer is mentioned anywhere. Cross-contamination risk is high given the small kitchen. Guests with celiac disease should contact the restaurant before visiting; some plain-grilled (白焼き) preparations may be lower-risk if shoyu can be omitted, but this requires direct confirmation.
seats_text: カウンター5席、テーブル6席、座敷24席（個室あり・最大20名の貸切対応）
cultural_comfort_note: A traditional Japanese anago specialty house with tatami seating and kaiseki-style courses; English menus are available but the experience and staff interactions are conducted primarily in Japanese.

bio.background: Hiiragi Honten is the flagship location of a small Himeji company (株式会社 HILO, representative director Hiroshi Tanaka) dedicated to Harima Himeji's prized conger eel. The restaurant opened in June 2005 along the Juniisho-mae-cho stretch near the castle, drawing on the rich fishing grounds of the Harima Strait — a fishery historically celebrated for producing especially plump, delicately flavoured anago. The brand later expanded to a second site inside the Qwell Himeji shopping complex, but the Honten retains the original intimate feel with tatami rooms, counter seats, and a still-small 35-seat layout. A predecessor establishment called 本家田中の焼あなご (Honke Tanaka's Grilled Anago) is referenced in early reviews, suggesting the current brand grew from an older family trade.
bio.origin: 姫路市十二所前町、播磨灘産穴子を専門とする
bio.specialty: 白焼き穴子（白焼き：素材の旨味を生かした塩焼き）および穴子づくし御膳（穴子の刺身・焼き・蒸し・天ぷら・飯の膳）
bio.philosophy: 播磨灘の新鮮な穴子を多彩な調理法で提供し、素材本来の風味を最大限に引き出す

anecdotes.text: Reviewers on 4travel single out the 穴子づくし御膳 (all-anago set) — featuring sashimi, white-grilled, steamed, tempura, and rice — as the definitive way to experience the range of Harima anago preparations in one sitting.
An early Tabelog reviewer references 本家田中の焼あなご as a predecessor, placing Hiiragi within a multi-generational local anago tradition in Himeji.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Tempura is a core menu item; wheat flour is used in the batter. No dedicated fryer or separate wheat-free cooking line is mentioned across any source reviewed.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard wheat-containing soy sauce is the default condiment and marinade base for grilled and simmered anago preparations. No tamari or gluten-free shoyu alternative is mentioned.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The entire menu is built around fish and seafood; all broths and sauces derive from anago and dashi. There is no plant-based cooking line.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy handling procedures or allergen menu are documented on any available source. Direct contact by phone (079-284-1239) before visiting is strongly advised for any dietary restriction.

positives.text: White-grilled anago (白焼き) is a minimal-ingredient preparation that showcases the eel without heavy saucing — if diners can confirm no shoyu marinade is applied and the piece is cooked on a clean surface, this could be the lowest-wheat item on the menu.

caveats: Cash-only at lunch is reported by one traveler reviewer (4travel) despite Tabelog listing credit cards accepted — verify on arrival or call ahead. Sunday and public holidays are dinner-only (no lunch service). Reservation recommended for evening courses; the restaurant can turn away walk-ins. Official website (anago-hiiragi.com) returned HTTP 403 and could not be directly scraped; information sourced from Tabelog, Retty, Gnavi, tanosu.com, and 4travel.

neighborhood: Honmachi, foot of Himeji Castle
address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市本町68
geocode_note: GSI geocoder returned [134.687363, 34.835873] for 兵庫県姫路市本町68番地. Distance to target center 531m, within 1.15km radius. Within target lat/lng box.
seats_text: 70 seats across 7 private rooms; one room seats up to 20, one has sunken kotatsu seating; second-floor room has Himeji Castle views (advance reservation required)
cultural_comfort_note: Formal traditional kaiseki setting with tatami and private rooms; the official site is Japanese-only and reservations must be made by phone in Japanese; the Tabelog English page provides basic info but staff interaction will require Japanese.
gf_detail: No gluten-free designation. Traditional kaiseki relies heavily on wheat-containing soy sauce (shoyu) and dashi made from bonito; suppon hot pot courses also typically include soy sauce in broth. The omakase kaiseki notes it can accommodate ingredient preferences and allergies when reserving by phone, but there is no evidence of dedicated GF protocols or wheat-free alternatives. Celiac diners should call ahead and ask specifically about shoyu substitution — cross-contamination risk is high in a traditional Japanese kitchen.
vegan_detail: No vegan options listed. The restaurant's identity centers on suppon (soft-shell turtle), seafood, and kaiseki featuring meat, fish, and bonito-based dashi. The menu can accept ingredient requests at reservation time, but a fully plant-based course would be a major departure from the kitchen's focus. Not a realistic vegan venue without direct negotiation in Japanese.

bio.background: The predecessor business, Shirakura Shokudo, was established in 1916. After World War II, the second-generation owner Kenji Shirakura — who had trained as a naval ship's cook — reopened as Hyotei in 1946 in Himeji's Gofukucho district. In 1958 the current main location at Honmachi 68, at the base of Himeji Castle, was established as Kappo Hyotei. The third generation, Houso Shirakura, underwent training at over 30 renowned restaurants across Japan before returning to lead the kitchen. In 2005 the fourth generation, Hiromitsu Shirakura (born 1972), took over, having himself trained at Japanese restaurants, ryokan, and Tokyo dining establishments from age 18 before assuming leadership at 33.
bio.specialty: Suppon (soft-shell turtle) hot pot — the restaurant's most celebrated dish. The suppon is sourced from Anjoin in Oita Prefecture and the broth is simmered for over 8 hours until it turns a distinctive milky-white color. The restaurant claims a 90% repeat-customer rate for this course. Seasonal additions include pike conger (hamo) in summer, pufferfish (fugu) in winter, wagyu sukiyaki, bear nabe, and Seto Inland Sea seafood. Exceptional or unusual requests — whale, wild boar, pheasant — can be sourced on request.
bio.philosophy: The current owner's stated mission is to 'deliver emotion through food' and to run a place where connections endure across 100–200 years: 語り継がれる瓢亭という想い出、受け継がれる瓢亭という場所 ('memories of Hyotei passed down through stories; Hyotei as a place inherited across time').

anecdotes.text: The restaurant began serving suppon dishes in 1975 and developed its original hot pot recipe in-house, building a following strong enough that roughly 9 in 10 customers who try the suppon course return for it.

The second-generation owner Kenji Shirakura drew on his experience cooking on naval ships during WWII when he reopened Hyotei in 1946, bringing professional kitchen discipline to what had been a family food business.
The second-floor private room offers a direct view of Himeji Castle and must be reserved specifically in advance — it is treated as a special occasion seat.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information on dedicated fryers or separated prep surfaces is publicly available. The kitchen handles fried suppon, tempura-style preparations, and other wheat-using techniques across a shared kitchen.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Traditional Japanese soy sauce (shoyu, which contains wheat) is a foundational ingredient in kaiseki cooking — used in broths, marinades, sauces, and finishing. No wheat-free soy sauce alternative is mentioned anywhere on the official site or review platforms.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All courses are built around animal proteins (suppon, seafood, game, wagyu). Dashi is almost certainly katsuobushi (bonito flake) or similar fish/animal-based stock. No plant-based dashi alternative is indicated.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The official menu page notes that the omakase kaiseki course 'accepts ingredient requests and accommodates allergies/preferences' — but this must be communicated at the time of phone reservation. There is no online form or written allergy protocol described.

positives.text: Fully private-room dining eliminates cross-contact from other tables and allows the chef to tailor each course to the group's stated needs.

caveats: Hours confirmed as Mon–Sat 17:00–22:00 (L.O. 21:30), closed Sunday, from both the official site and Tabelog/Hitosara. Seat count discrepancy: Tabelog lists 70 seats across 7 private rooms; Hitosara lists 35 people capacity — the 70 likely reflects total seats while 35 is the maximum for a single booking. Reservation by phone only per official site (079-222-8180); the Tabelog English page suggests online reservation but the official site specifies phone only. Price range ¥11,000–¥19,999 per person for dinner courses. The hyoutei.com domain appears to be the parent company site (also operates a branch in Nishi-Nikaichomachi); himeji-suppon.com is specifically for the Honmachi main branch (本店).

name_ja: 播州姫路本町たまごや
address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市本町68 家老屋敷館「は」の屋敷内
geocode_note: GSI AddressSearch on 兵庫県姫路市本町68 returned [134.687363, 34.835873]. Approximately 530m from target center (34.8331, 134.6921) — well within the 1.15km radius.
neighborhood: 本町 (家老屋敷館 / 姫路城前)
seats_text: 20 seats (6 counter seats, approx 4 tables); all non-smoking
hours_raw: 11:00〜17:00（L.O. 16:30）年中無休（年末年始・臨時休業あり）
closed_days: No regular closing day; closed Dec 31–Jan 1 and occasional temporary closures (Golden Week, Obon periods sometimes affected)
cultural_comfort_note: English and Chinese menus are available; the experience is welcoming to tourists visiting Himeji Castle, and staff have been noted as helpful to dietary queries.
vegan_detail: The entire menu is built around eggs (tamago kake gohan, thick omelette, conger eel and egg). Miso soup is included with sets and likely uses katsuobushi (bonito) dashi in the Harima red-miso broth. No plant-based alternatives are offered. Egg-eating pescatarians could find the plain rice-and-egg base workable, but strict vegans have nothing here.
gf_detail: The core dish — plain rice plus raw egg — is inherently wheat-free, but the standard set comes with wheat-containing elements: (1) the house original soy sauce (tare) is wheat-brewed standard shoyu; (2) bonito flakes are sprinkled on rice by default and must be requested omitted; (3) the Harima red-miso soup's dashi and miso paste are unconfirmed for wheat. This is not a dedicated GF facility. Reviewers on FindMeGlutenFree describe staff as accommodating when asked, and one celiac diner ate rice and eggs safely after communicating needs, but also noted the need to bring personal GF soy sauce. Cross-contamination risk from the kitchen cannot be ruled out.
caveats: Hours listed as daily 11:00–17:00 but the restaurant notes irregular closures during Golden Week, Obon, and around New Year — verify before visiting. No card payments accepted. A touch-panel queue system operates outside during busy periods (peak tourist season).

bio.background: The restaurant is run by Fujihashi Shoten, a Himeji egg-farming family whose chicken operations date to 1925. They raise approximately 350,000 birds across two farms in the naturally rich districts of Yumesaki and Shikitou on the outskirts of Himeji, using custom-blended feed and — by their own account — playing Mozart for the flock. The Himeji Castle-front shop opened in 2009 as a way to bring those farm eggs directly to the table.
bio.specialty: Tamakake meshi — plain cooked rice served with one of two house egg varieties and a drizzle of original blended soy sauce (tare co-developed with a local shoyu maker), with unlimited refills of both rice and eggs. The premium tier features Yume-oh eggs, which won three consecutive national Tamago Kake Gohan Festival competitions and were retired to the hall of fame.
bio.philosophy: Farm-to-bowl simplicity: grow your own chickens, mill your own rice, develop your own soy sauce, and let the quality speak. Fujihashi Shoten sources nearly all ingredients from its own farms or from local Harima-region producers.
bio.origin: Himeji, Hyogo Prefecture, Japan — inside the Karoyashiki (historic samurai retainer compound) at Honmachi 68, directly on the approach to Himeji Castle.

anecdotes.text: The premium 'Yume-oh' egg competed in the national Tamago Kake Gohan Festival three times and won each time, eventually being inducted into the festival's hall of fame and retired from competition.
The chickens on the Fujihashi farms are raised while listening to Mozart — a detail the restaurant itself highlights as part of its low-stress farming philosophy.

A touch-panel ticketing screen outside the shop lets waiting diners take a number and browse the nearby souvenir stalls before their seat is ready — a small quality-of-life touch noted by multiple visitors.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Not a dedicated gluten-free facility; kitchen cross-contamination risk is unconfirmed. No allergen separation policy is published.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The house original soy sauce (tare) is a custom wheat-brewed shoyu co-developed with a local manufacturer. It is not gluten-free. Coeliacs should bring their own tamari or GF soy sauce.
One FindMeGlutenFree reviewer specifically advises bringing your own soy sauce to this restaurant.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Bonito flakes (katsuo-bushi) are sprinkled on rice by default; must be requested omitted. Miso soup dashi type is unconfirmed but likely katsuobushi-based given regional Harima red-miso tradition.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Reviewers on FindMeGlutenFree describe staff as helpful and accommodating when dietary needs are communicated clearly. One celiac diner reported eating rice, eggs, and pickled plums safely after asking about ingredients.

positives.text: The core ingredients — farm-fresh raw egg over plain white rice — are naturally wheat-free and suitable as a baseline for gluten-avoiders who communicate clearly with staff.

cuisine: Donburi, Udon, Himeji Oden, Shirasu (whitebait), Anago (conger eel)
address_ja: 〒670-0012 兵庫県姫路市本町68-290 イーグレひめじ1F
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI AddressSearch for 兵庫県姫路市本町68番地; coordinates fall within target box (lat 34.823–34.843, lng 134.679–134.705), approximately 0.3 km from the Himeji Castle–Station midpoint.
neighborhood: 本町 / イーグレひめじ / 姫路城徒歩3分
hours_raw: 11:00〜14:30（料理L.O. 14:30）、夕方以降は団体のお客様のみ・要予約。年末年始のみ不定休。
seats_text: テーブル席40、お座敷20、最大70名まで対応可
cultural_comfort_note: Tourist-facing venue steps from Himeji Castle with multilingual menus and international visitor services; staff are accustomed to foreign guests.
vegan_detail: The menu is built around seafood — shirasu (whitebait), anago (conger eel), and ebi (shrimp tempura) — with dashi-based udon soups and Himeji oden. No plant-based alternatives are listed, and hidden fish stock (dashi/katsuo) is near-certain in soups and oden broth. Not suitable for vegans.
gf_detail: Wheat is pervasive: udon (wheat noodles), tempura batter on shrimp and conger eel, and soy sauce used throughout (including the house oyster-dashi soy sauce). The signature shirasu don sets include somen or udon as side dishes. No gluten-free menu, no GF soy sauce alternative, and no dedicated fryer is mentioned. Not suitable for celiac diners.

bio.background: 千姫茶屋 is operated by 輝光ライブフード株式会社 (Kiko Live Food Co., Ltd.), a Himeji-based hospitality company that also runs the Michelin-listed kaiseki counter おもてなしきこう and the tonkatsu specialty restaurant 別邸 谷かつ. The chaya (tea house) concept sits at the more accessible, tourist-friendly end of the group's portfolio — intended to give visitors from afar not just a meal but a taste of Himeji memory-making.
bio.specialty: Shirasu donburi: fresh Harima Nada whitebait served in an oversized bowl shaped to resemble the helmet of local warlord Kuroda Kanbei, alongside Himeji oden (eaten with ginger soy sauce) and Banshu Ibonoito somen noodles.
bio.philosophy: To offer travelers an experience of Himeji's flavours and hospitality — cuisine as souvenir of place, not merely sustenance.
roles: hospitality group (輝光ライブフード株式会社)

anecdotes.text: The signature shirasu bowl arrives in a 30 cm black vessel modelled on warlord Kuroda Kanbei's helmet — a theatrical presentation that turns a bowl of whitebait into a piece of Himeji history.

The whitebait comes from the shallow, fast-flowing waters of Harima Nada, fed by five rivers whose nutrients produce fish with notably firm flesh and clean flavour, according to the restaurant's own description.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Tempura (shrimp, conger eel) is a core menu item, meaning wheat flour is in active use in the kitchen; no dedicated fryer or separate prep area is mentioned.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The house condiment is an oyster-dashi soy sauce (東丸のカキだし醤油) — a wheat-containing soy sauce used at table and almost certainly in cooking. No tamari or GF soy sauce alternative is listed.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Fish stock (dashi) is the base of udon soups and oden broth; shirasu, anago, and ebi are prepared throughout the kitchen. No plant-only cooking area exists.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The restaurant notes halal accommodation is available upon special arrangement, suggesting some dietary flexibility, but no mention of gluten or allergen handling protocols appears on any public source.

positives.text: Credit cards accepted (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, JCB, Diners), multilingual menus available, and barrier-free access including wheelchair and sign language services — logistics are well handled even if dietary accommodation is limited.

caveats: Hours data shows some variation across sources (11:00–14:30 on most; one source lists 11:00–15:00). The 14:30 last-order time from the official owst.jp site and HotPepper is treated as authoritative. Evening service exists only for groups by prior reservation. The restaurant is not independent — it is part of the 輝光ライブフード株式会社 group — but remains a small, Himeji-specific local operator rather than a national chain.

address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市駅前町301
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 兵庫県姫路市駅前町301番地 returned [134.692093, 34.829098]. Distance from target center (34.8331, 134.6921) is approximately 0.44km — well within the 1.15km radius.
neighborhood: 駅前町 / JR姫路駅北口徒歩3分
seats_text: カウンター7席のみ。一見狭いが回転が早く、開店前から行列ができることも。
cultural_comfort_note: カウンター7席のみの小さな老舗で、英語メニューや英語対応スタッフは期待できない。注文は指差しでも可能。
vegan_detail: とんかつ（豚ヒレ・ロース）、オムライス（豚肉入り）が主力。料理全般に豚肉・卵・乳製品を使用し、ヴィーガン対応メニューは存在しない。みそ汁ベースのとん汁も動物性だしが主体。
gf_detail: とんかつは小麦粉パン粉で揚げた料理であり、グルテンフリー対応は不可能。とん汁の味噌に小麦が含まれる可能性があり、オムライスのケチャップライスも含め小麦リスクが全品に及ぶ。専用フライヤーの有無は不明だが、パン粉揚げ物専門店のため交差汚染は避けられない。

bio.background: 創業82年以上の姫路の老舗とんかつ店。店名「赤心」は、創業者の曽祖父が尊敬したとされる勝海舟の言葉に由来するといわれ、「誠実な心」を意味する。かつては姫路市内に4店舗を展開していたが、現在は本店1軒のみを営業。テレビや各種メディアに複数回取り上げられた実績を持つ。
bio.specialty: とんかつ定食（パン粉揚げのロース・ヒレカツ）と、とろける玉ねぎと濃厚味噌のとん汁。オムライスは鶏肉ではなく豚肉を使用するのが特徴。
bio.philosophy: 長年変わらない味を守り続けるスタイル。セルフ感のない丁寧なカウンター対応。

anecdotes.text: 開店15分前から並んでも2巡目待ちになることがある人気ぶり。著者はとん汁目当てに訪問し「とろけそうな玉ねぎの甘味とコクのある味噌でやみつきになる味」と記している。
創業82年で姫路の老舗として行列ができる店と紹介され、過去4店舗から現在1店舗に集約した経緯が記されている。

gf_cross_contamination.text: とんかつ専門店のため、揚げ油は小麦パン粉が常時混入しており、セリアック病・小麦アレルギーの方には極めて高リスク。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: とん汁・とんかつソース・ドレッシングなど複数のアイテムに小麦含有醤油またはソースが使われている可能性が高いが、公式な成分情報は公開されていない。

vegan_cross_contact.text: 豚肉・卵・乳製品が全メニューに使用されており、ヴィーガン向けの交差汚染回避は不可能。

staff_allergy_handling.text: アレルギー対応に関する情報は公式・ブログいずれからも確認できず、現金のみ・予約不可の小規模店のためアレルギー相談体制は期待できない。

caveats: 営業時間はTabelog公式（11:00-15:00、火水金土日）を採用。一部ブログには18:00閉店の記述があるが、現在のTabelog掲載情報を優先した。創業年数（82年）はブログ記事（2023年頃執筆）基準のため現時点では84〜85年程度の可能性あり。とん汁の閉店時間前に売り切れることがあるとの口コミあり。

cuisine: アーモンドバター専門カフェ
address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市本町68 大手前第一ビル1F
geocode_note: Geocoded from 兵庫県姫路市本町68 via GSI AddressSearch API; coordinates [134.687363, 34.835873] place the café approximately 0.5 km from target center — well within the 1.15 km radius.
neighborhood: 本町 (大手前・姫路城前)
seats_text: 15席（カウンター7席、2人掛けテーブル4卓）
hours_raw: 月火木金土日 11:00-17:00（フードL.O.16:00、ドリンクL.O.16:30）、水曜定休。2026年3月より金・土曜は夜カフェ営業追加。
cultural_comfort_note: Manager Sugahara noted a specific desire to engage with foreign visitors who come to see Himeji Castle, and the café is actively developing takeout menus for walking tourists.
vegan_detail: No vegan options confirmed. The core menu is built around butter — homemade dairy-based almond butter on wheat toast — plus almond milk lattes that likely include dairy. The butter chicken curry uses dairy. No plant-based substitutions or vegan-labeled items found in any source reviewed.
gf_detail: No gluten-free options confirmed. The signature dish is almond butter toast on wheat bread. The butter chicken curry uses a roux made with 18 spices (likely wheat-thickened). Standard soy sauce is common in Japanese café kitchens. No GF-labeled items, no dedicated fryer information, and no allergy accommodation language found in any source.

bio.background: Opened November 28, 2025 in Otemae Daiichi Building 1F, directly south of Ōtemae Park with a full view of Himeji Castle.
bio.specialty: 自家製アーモンドバタートーストセット — homemade almond butter in two styles (smooth Classic and crunchy Crunch), roasted and ground in-house, served on thick toast with almond-milk soup and bagna cauda-style almond sauce dip.
bio.philosophy: Manager Sugahara set out to elevate Himeji's well-known almond butter local-specialty tradition from souvenir culture into a craft café experience. The café grinds its own almonds and blends almond into as many menu items as possible — lattes, curry, spice toppings — while welcoming the international tourists who visit the castle next door.

anecdotes.text: From March 2026, the café began Friday and Saturday evening hours so guests could enjoy the illuminated Himeji Castle visible through the front windows.
The butter chicken curry is finished with dukkah — an Egyptian spice blend that incorporates almonds — connecting almond to global culinary traditions.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No soy sauce information found in any source; standard Japanese soy sauce contains wheat and is common in café kitchens. Assume wheat-containing soy sauce unless confirmed otherwise.

caveats: Hours are irregular: standard daytime hours are Mon/Tue/Thu-Sun 11:00-17:00, but as of March 2026 Friday and Saturday also have evening service (hours not confirmed). Night hours should be verified before visiting. No vegan or GF options were found in any source — this café is not appropriate for celiac diners or strict vegans. The Tabelog listing was tagged "ビーガン対応カフェ" in the task brief, but no evidence of vegan-specific menus or labeling was found during research; the classification appears to be erroneous or aspirational.

cuisine: Izakaya / Conger eel (anago) / Himeji local specialties
address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市南町55
geocode_note: GSI AddressSearch on 兵庫県姫路市南町55 returned [134.690338, 34.829132]. Within target box and approximately 0.4 km from center point — confirmed in radius.
neighborhood: 南町 (Minami-cho) — 2-minute walk north of Sanyo-Himeji Station
seats_text: 37 seats total (counter, sunken kotatsu-style tables, semi-private partition seating); 2nd-floor private room for up to 22. Some sources cite 33 seats for main floor.
hours_raw: Tue–Thu, Sun: 17:00–23:00 (LO 22:30); Fri–Sat: 17:00–24:00 (LO 23:30); Closed Monday. Hours subject to change.
closed_days: 月曜日（月曜が祝日の場合は営業の場合あり）
cultural_comfort_note: Counter and table-style izakaya with Japanese-language menus and staff; basic Japanese or phone reservations expected, no English menu confirmed.
vegan_detail: No vegan menu or plant-based options advertised. Core identity is conger eel (anago) and seasonal seafood with Japanese dashi stock, which typically contains bonito (katsuobushi) and/or kelp. Hidden animal products in sauces, dashi, and marinades are near-certain. Advance inquiry in Japanese is essential.
gf_detail: Standard izakaya with wheat-containing soy sauce (shoyu) used throughout — in marinades, dressings, and dipping sauces. Oden broth and many sauces are likely wheat-based. No gluten-free menu or allergen labeling advertised. Cross-contamination risk is high in a shared kitchen. Dedicated fryer status unknown. Advance inquiry in Japanese is required; celiac diners should exercise caution.

bio.background: A locally rooted izakaya in Minami-cho, a short walk from Sanyo-Himeji Station, built around Himeji's culinary heritage. The shop has positioned itself as a showcase for Himeji's most distinctive local ingredients and traditions.
bio.specialty: Densuké anago (伝助穴子) — a rare variety of conger eel representing only a few percent of all eel catches — served as sashimi, grilled, and in hitsumabushi-style preparations. Also features hineppon (ひねぽん, a Himeji chicken specialty), Himeji lotus root, and Himeji oden.
bio.philosophy: The concept centers on celebrating the Banshu (western Hyogo) food culture: locally sourced Seto Inland Sea seafood, Himeji specialties, and regional sake labels such as Tatsuriki (龍力) and Yaegaki (八重垣), presented in a calm, contemporary Japanese aesthetic.

anecdotes.text: The restaurant's own site describes the interior as 'a separate world' upon entry — a modern space where floor-level windows reveal garden-like designs, blending old and new Japanese aesthetics.
Densuké anago is described as accounting for only a few percent of all anago caught, making it a prized local delicacy central to Tsunagi's menu identity.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Standard izakaya kitchen with no GF segregation advertised. Wheat-based soy sauce and dashi are core to virtually every dish. Cross-contamination risk is high.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Traditional Japanese soy sauce (含む小麦) is the backbone of Japanese izakaya cooking and almost certainly used throughout the menu — in marinades, tare, dipping sauces, and oden broth. No wheat-free shoyu or tamari alternatives are mentioned.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Bonito-based (katsuobushi) dashi is standard in Japanese izakaya broth, sauces, and oden. Seafood is central to the menu concept. No plant-based preparation stream exists.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergen menu, allergy handling protocol, or foreign-language dietary communication noted on any source. Direct inquiry by phone in Japanese is the only confirmed route.

positives.text: Cards widely accepted (Visa, MC, Amex, JCB, Diners, e-money); counter seating available for solo diners; reservations accepted by phone and online.

caveats: Hours show inconsistency across sources: Tabelog lists Mon closed / Tue–Sun open; official site and Hotpepper list Sun closed / Mon–Sat open; Gnavi distinguishes Fri–Sat extended hours to midnight. Hours_week above uses Tabelog as primary with Gnavi's Fri–Sat extension. Always verify before visiting. Seat count also varies (33–37) across listings. Under-21 guests not permitted per multiple sources; smoking throughout the venue.

name_ja: 天空・地酒・おもてなし きこう×龍力
cuisine: 日本料理・懐石・創作和食
address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市本町68-290 イーグレひめじ4F
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 兵庫県姫路市本町68番地 returned [134.687363, 34.835873]. Approximately 0.53 km from target center — well within the 1.15 km radius.
neighborhood: 本町・イーグレひめじ（姫路城前）
seats_text: 着席約50席（テーブル席42席）、VIP席・個室・カップル席あり。立食は別途相談。
cultural_comfort_note: 高級懐石コース専門店でメニューは日本語中心。スタッフは英語対応の記載なし。外国人向けメニューカスタマイズ（神戸牛コースなど）は応相談とあるが、要事前連絡。
vegan_detail: コースは鰹だし・醤油・魚介を多用する正統派懐石。ヴィーガン対応の明示なし。ハラール対応可能との記載があり食材変更の応相談の余地はあるが、隠し出汁（鰹・昆布）の省略や完全植物性コースへの変更は事前問い合わせ必須。
gf_detail: 懐石料理に醤油（小麦含有）・天ぷら衣・揚げ物が広く使われる。グルテンフリー対応の記載は一切なく、専用フライヤーの有無も不明。セリアック対応は保証できず、訪問前に店舗へ直接確認が必須。

bio.chef_name: 安田忠正（やすだ ただまさ）
bio.background: 1968年兵庫県生まれ。高校・大学時代から飲食業に携わり、ホテル勤務でプロ野球選手向けの食事を担当。「作り置きした料理は感動を与えられない」という確信を得て独立。姫路の食材を知り尽くした地元出身者として、旬の瀬戸内・但馬食材を活かした料理で「わざわざ来る価値のある店」を目指す。兵庫県特産品振興協会会長も務める。
bio.specialty: 旬の食材を使ったおまかせ懐石コース（¥11,000〜¥22,000）。地元・姫路の清酒「龍力」との特別ペアリング。
bio.philosophy: 「目の前に世界文化遺産を望める景色に負けない料理で、おもてなしをしたい」——姫路城の絶景に見合う料理と空間で、訪れる人を感動させることを信条とする。

anecdotes.text: ホテル勤務時代にプロ野球選手向けの食事を担当。時間が経った料理は美味しくないと痛感し、「お客様の目の前で調理するライブクッキング」を自店の核心に据えた。
「ミシュランガイド兵庫2016」に掲載。食材選びと仕込みへの妥協のなさが評価された。
2024年7月、創業100年を超える姫路の酒蔵「龍力（本田商店）」とコラボし、イーグレひめじ4階に天空レストランをオープン。他では入手できない特別な龍力の酒を提供する。

gf_cross_contamination.text: 天ぷら・揚げ物を含む懐石コースで専用フライヤーの有無は不明。グルテン交差汚染リスクに関する情報は一切公開されていない。
