soy_sauce_wheat.text: 懐石料理では醤油（小麦含有）が煮物・焼き物・たれ類に幅広く使われる。グルテンフリー醤油（丸大豆醤油・タマリ）使用の記載なし。

vegan_cross_contact.text: コースは魚介・鰹だしを基本とする。完全植物性メニューへの対応を示す情報はなく、だし・ソースへの動物性素材の混入リスクが高い。

staff_allergy_handling.text: ハラール・ムスリム対応可能との記載あり（公式サイト）。アレルギー一般については「事前にご相談ください」の案内のみで、具体的な対応プロセスの開示なし。

positives.text: ハラール対応を公式に表明しており、食材・調理への配慮ができるスタッフ体制がある程度整っていると推測される。
おまかせコースは事前相談でカスタマイズ可能（海外からのゲスト向けに神戸牛コース等へ変更実績あり）。食制限も事前連絡で応相談の余地はある。

caveats: 営業時間は各曜日の差異が未確認のため全日同一として記載。不定休のため訪問前に電話（079-240-8457）またはウェブ予約で確認要。席数はTabelog（50席）と公式サイト（40席）で表記に差異あり。GF・ヴィーガン対応の具体的な可否は店舗への直接問い合わせなしには確認不可。

address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市福沢町171
geocode_note: GSI address-search returned lat 34.830994, lng 134.68396 for 兵庫県姫路市福沢町１７１番地; 779 m from target center (34.8331, 134.6921) — well within 1.15 km.
neighborhood: 福沢町 (Fukusawa-cho), ~10 min walk west of Himeji / Sanyo-Himeji Station
vegan_detail: Several menu items are naturally plant-based: housemade yuba (tofu skin) sashimi set, doujiang (Taiwanese soy milk soup), soy milk soup curry with multigrain rice, and soy milk okara donuts. However, the lunch menu also includes a salt-koji chicken set, so this is not a dedicated vegan establishment. No mention of hidden dashi or bonito in the soy-based dishes has appeared in any source, but the restaurant does not explicitly label vegan items and uses conventional Japanese pantry ingredients — fish-based dashi in soups cannot be ruled out without asking staff.
gf_detail: The core product range is soy and tofu, which are naturally gluten-free, but the menu includes wheat-containing items (toast, soy milk soup curry base may use standard soy sauce). Conventional Japanese soy sauce (shoyu) contains wheat — there is no published claim of tamari or GF shoyu use here. The doujiang soup and yuba dishes may be safe depending on dashi and seasoning choices, but no dedicated GF protocol or allergen menu has been confirmed. Cross-contamination risk exists in a small single-operator kitchen. Ask staff directly before ordering.
hours_raw: Tue–Sat 09:00–15:00 (L.O. 14:30); closed Sun, Mon, and public holidays. Morning service 09:00–11:00; Lunch service 11:00–15:00.
seats_text: 19 seats on the ground floor; second floor available as a rental space for events and meetings
cultural_comfort_note: A small, solo-run neighbourhood café in a quiet residential-commercial area west of the station; no English signage or menu has been reported, so basic Japanese or a translation app is recommended.

bio.background: Housed in a converted warehouse belonging to Umezawa Ryokoku Co., Ltd. (梅澤糧穀株式会社), a grain wholesale business founded in 1920 that has supplied soybeans and grains across Himeji for over a century. An earlier iteration of the restaurant operated at the same Fukusawa-cho address but closed; in July 2024 the owner's daughter revived it, initially running the entire operation — cooking, counter service, and sourcing — on her own.
bio.specialty: Housemade yuba (fresh tofu skin) sashimi set and doujiang (台湾式豆漿) — a Taiwanese-style soy milk soup curdled with vinegar and finished with chilli oil. Soybeans are the 'Yume-hana' (夢華) variety grown without pesticides in Yumesaki-cho, prized for their large grain and concentrated sweetness.
bio.philosophy: 自然の力をまるごと、誠実に — 'Capturing the full force of nature, honestly.' The kitchen avoids artificial processing and draws directly on the wholesale company's access to premium-grade legumes to put the intrinsic flavour of the bean at the centre of every dish.

anecdotes.text: The restaurant reopened on 27 July 2024 after a nine-year closure, with the founder's daughter managing everything solo from the kitchen to front-of-house during the initial launch phase.

The doujiang on the menu is a Taiwanese breakfast staple unfamiliar to many Japanese diners: soy milk soup whose protein is curdled by vinegar, topped with chilli oil — a deliberate nod to the bean's culinary breadth beyond traditional Japanese tofu.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No dedicated GF kitchen or fryer has been confirmed. This is a small single-operator café with a mixed menu including wheat-containing toast and possibly standard soy sauce — cross-contamination cannot be excluded.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No source confirms the use of tamari or wheat-free soy sauce. Standard Japanese shoyu (which contains wheat) is the default assumption for all cooked dishes. Ask staff to confirm the specific shoyu used before ordering.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The lunch menu includes a salt-koji chicken set alongside plant-based dishes. In a small kitchen run by one person, shared prep surfaces and cookware are likely. No vegan-specific protocol has been published.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergen menu, staff allergy-handling policy, or dedicated allergy procedure has been mentioned in any published source. Direct communication with the owner-operator (who also cooks) is the recommended approach — phone 080-9475-9197.

positives.text: The restaurant's entire identity revolves around minimally processed soybean and tofu cookery, with house-sourced 'Yume-hana' soybeans grown without pesticides — a cleaner ingredient baseline than a general restaurant.
The proprietor runs the kitchen solo, meaning ingredient questions go directly to the person who cooked the food — no intermediary needed.

caveats: Hours and closed-day data sourced from Tabelog and official site as of July 2024 reopening; verify before visiting as a solo-operator café may adjust seasonally. No English menu confirmed — Japanese communication or translation app recommended. GF and vegan safety assessments are based on publicly available menu descriptions only; no allergen declaration or dedicated protocol has been published by the restaurant.

neighborhood: 本町 (Honmachi)
address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市本町68 平和不動産ビル1階西
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 兵庫県姫路市本町68 returned [134.687363, 34.835873]; approximately 530m from the Himeji Castle–Station midpoint, well within the 1.15km radius.
closed_days: 月曜、第2火曜（祝日の場合は翌日休み）
seats_text: カウンター・テーブル合わせて約22席。昼時は満席になりやすい。
cultural_comfort_note: Casual neighbourhood bakery with a small eat-in area; no English menu but pointing at items on the counter works fine, and staff are accustomed to tourists visiting from Himeji Castle.
vegan_detail: Most of the natural-yeast bagels are made without eggs or dairy (the plain bagel allergen list shows only wheat), making them the clearest vegan option. Many flavoured bagels, however, include cream cheese, butter, or honey, and sourdough loaves often contain dairy. Several sourdough breads are egg-free but include dairy. Burgers use beef and standard buns; no vegan burger option is listed. Always confirm individually at the counter, as the allergen label per item is your best guide.
gf_detail: This is a wheat-specialist bakery: every product is made with wheat flour (100% Hokkaido "Haruyu Taka Blend" for bagels; wheat also in all sourdough and pastry items). No gluten-free products are offered and there is no dedicated gluten-free preparation area. Not suitable for anyone with celiac disease or a gluten intolerance.

bio.background: Kawahara Aya originally trained in cosmetology before pivoting to the food industry, working in restaurants where she built skills in cooking and desserts. She then moved into bread-making, discovering that it let her apply every culinary skill she had accumulated. She and her husband made repeated research trips to the United States to study New York-style hamburgers before opening their Himeji shop in April 2010.
bio.origin: Opened April 2010 in a formerly disused building just off Otemae-dori, a 5-minute walk from Himeji Castle. A rainbow umbrella marks the alley entrance. The name 燈 (lamp) reflects Kawahara's wish to create a place with the warmth and glow of a lantern — 'a space where customers feel excited.'
bio.specialty: Natural-yeast bagels (20+ rotating varieties) made with 100% Hokkaido 'Haruyu Taka Blend' wheat and Hoshino natural yeast for a characteristic chewiness; and NY-style hamburgers featuring coarse-ground Australian beef patties on housemade buns.
bio.philosophy: Kawahara's core principle is 'Make bread that I want to eat myself.' She sources seasonal vegetables from young local Himeji farmers, sometimes commissioning specific crops. She experiments with fermented ingredients including kefir and house-cultivated yeast starters, and prioritises bringing out the quality of each ingredient over complexity.

anecdotes.text: Kawahara notes that hearing 'delicious!' from customers is what sustains her through the hardships of running the bakery — 'every time I hear it, I feel saved.'
The shop's proprietor reportedly declined to be photographed for a local Himeji blog article ahead of opening the second location, but was described by the writer as 'notably handsome.'

gf_cross_contamination.text: Every item in the shop is made with wheat flour. There is no gluten-free preparation area or gluten-free product line. Cross-contamination with wheat is a certainty throughout the kitchen and counter.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No soy sauce is listed as an ingredient in any bread or bagel product; the primary allergen across all items is wheat flour itself.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Bagels are baked alongside dairy-containing varieties in the same oven. Plain and some unflavoured bagels may share trays with cheese or butter bagels. No dedicated vegan production line is indicated.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No published allergy protocol or staff allergy-training information was found. Each product does carry an allergen label on the website, which is the only formal allergen disclosure available. Visitors with serious allergies should ask staff to point to the relevant label.

positives.text: Most plain natural-yeast bagels list only wheat in their allergen declaration — no eggs or dairy — making them one of the clearer egg-free and dairy-free options in Himeji for non-celiac visitors.

caveats: Hours on the official site (10:00-18:00 daily) differ slightly from Tabelog (Tue-Sat until 19:00, Sun until 18:00). The Tabelog data appeared more detailed and aligns with blog reviews citing 19:00 closing on weekdays. Closed days are listed as Monday everywhere, but the second Tuesday closure appears on some sources and not others — treat Thursday as a possible additional closure day per the official site. Confirm before visiting. Vegan status of individual items requires checking the allergen label on each product; the information above is based on published website allergen declarations which may not reflect daily specials.

name_ja: 釜焼鳥本舗 おやひなや 姫路駅前店
address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市駅前町294 B1F
geocode_note: GSI geocode on 兵庫県姫路市駅前町２９４番地 returned 34.828522, 134.692139 — within target box and ~0.5 km from JR Himeji Station.
neighborhood: 駅前町 — JR姫路駅北口から徒歩3分
cuisine: 骨付き鳥 (釜焼き), 香川郷土料理, 居酒屋
seats_text: 52席（ロフト式座敷最大15名、最大予約38名）
hours_raw: 13:00〜22:00（食べログ掲載、2026年5月開店時点）。別情報源では11:00〜22:00・日曜定休とする記述もあり要確認。
closed_days: 不定休（食べログでは定休日なしと記載。一部情報源では日曜定休との記述もあり店舗へ要確認）
cultural_comfort_note: English, Chinese, and Korean menus are available, and the casual izakaya format is accessible to non-Japanese speakers, though staff may have limited English conversation ability.
vegan_detail: The entire menu is built around bone-in chicken. No plant-based mains exist, and chicken fat and drippings are integral to the cooking method. No vegan or vegetarian options are listed or confirmed.
gf_detail: This is a meat-focused izakaya with no stated gluten-free options. The chain also serves Sanuki udon (wheat noodles), creating high cross-contamination risk. Standard Japanese soy sauce (contains wheat) is used in the izakaya side dishes. No dedicated fryer information confirmed. Not safe for celiac diners.

bio.background: 釜焼鳥本舗 おやひなやは、関西を拠点に複数業態を展開するForward ball合同会社が運営する骨付き鳥専門居酒屋チェーン。姫路駅前店は2026年5月13日（水）にオープン。香川県丸亀市発祥の郷土料理「骨付鳥」を全国に広める目的で展開されており、姫路を含む各地に店舗を拡大している。
bio.origin: 香川県丸亀市。骨付鳥は1953年に丸亀の居酒屋「一鶴」の創業者・近藤貞一・多鶴子夫妻がハリウッド映画のローストチキンシーンからヒントを得て考案した料理が発祥とされる。
bio.specialty: 骨付鳥「釜焼き」。親鶏（180日以上の成鶏）と若鶏（60日未満）の2種を提供。秘伝のスパイスで仕込み、専用釜でじっくり焼き上げる「釜焼き」製法が特徴。スパイス調合はミリグラム単位で厳格に管理され、全店で味の一貫性を保つ。
bio.philosophy: 高級地鶏などの銘柄鶏にこだわらず、各地域で入手できる鶏を使うことで地域ごとの多様な解釈を大切にする。「素材より技」という姿勢で、地元の食材資源を香川の産地から奪わないよう意識した運営方針をとる。ペアリング推奨の日本酒「讃岐くらうでぃ」（白麹三倍量の特別な濁り酒）を各店で提供。

anecdotes.text: 「骨付鳥」の起源は1953年に丸亀市の一鶴で生まれた。創業者夫妻がハリウッド映画のローストチキンに着想を得て独自開発した料理が、現在は香川県を代表する郷土料理として全国に普及している。
開店から間もない2026年5月の訪問者レビューでは「皮はパリッと香ばしく焼き上げられており、ガブリとかぶりつくと中から溢れ出す肉汁と鶏の強烈な旨味がたまらない」と5点満点の高評価が付けられた。

gf_cross_contamination.text: The chain serves Sanuki udon (wheat noodles) alongside the chicken menu. A shared kitchen environment with wheat-based noodles presents a significant cross-contamination risk for celiac diners.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No tamari or wheat-free soy sauce is mentioned. Standard Japanese soy sauce containing wheat is almost certainly used in izakaya side dishes and marinades throughout the menu.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Chicken fat and drippings are integral to the signature dish; the entire concept is meat-centered with no plant-based menu items or vegan accommodation noted anywhere.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy handling procedures, allergen menus, or staff training for dietary restrictions were mentioned on any official or third-party source reviewed.

positives.text: English, Chinese, and Korean menus are available, making it easier to communicate basic preferences — though allergy communication in a foreign language remains the diner's responsibility.

caveats: Hours conflict: Tabelog (primary source, used for hours_week) lists 13:00-22:00 open daily; an opening announcement site lists 11:00-22:00 closed Sundays. The restaurant opened May 13, 2026 and hours may still be settling — call ahead to confirm. The official_url points to the Umeda branch (brand reference); no dedicated Himeji official site was found. The chain is operated by Forward ball LLC (multi-concept Kansai operator), not an independent family business.

address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市坂元町35
geocode_note: GSI AddressSearch matched 兵庫県姫路市坂元町３５番地; coordinates 134.68634/34.833492 fall within target box and well within 1.15km of 34.8331/134.6921.
neighborhood: 魚町・山陽姫路駅近
seats_text: 45 seats across fully private tatami rooms (8-person x2, 4-person x2, plus larger private rooms for up to 20+) and a 6-seat counter. No shared seating.
closed_days: 不定休（年末年始・お盆期間に休業あり、要確認）
cultural_comfort_note: Fully private tatami rooms, Japanese-language menus; staff are attentive but English communication is limited — phone ahead or use a translation app for special dietary requests.
vegan_detail: This is an omakase-style sushi and kaiseki restaurant built around fish, shellfish, and Setouchi conger eel. Dashi is almost certainly katsuobushi- or niboshi-based; standard shoyu likely contains wheat. There are no advertised vegan courses and no public mention of plant-based adaptation. A diner with strict vegan requirements should call ahead in Japanese; partial accommodation (e.g., vegetable dishes from the seasonal menu) may be possible but cannot be assumed.
gf_detail: Traditional sushi and kaiseki means wheat is present throughout: wheat-based shoyu on nigiri and in dashi-tare, flour-thickened sauces (nitsume/tare on the signature anago), and potential tempura on kaiseki courses. The restaurant has never been marketed as gluten-free and publishes no allergen matrix. There is no dedicated GF fryer confirmed. A celiac diner must contact the restaurant in advance (phone 079-222-6961, in Japanese) to discuss tamari substitution and wheat-free dashi; success is not guaranteed.

bio.background: Born 22 January 1975 in Himeji and raised partly in nature-rich Shisou City, Hyogo, Hisamune Ryuichi is the second-generation owner of すし一, which his family founded in 1970 (Showa 45). After training at respected restaurants in Kyoto and Kobe for more than two decades, he returned to Himeji to take over the family counter.
bio.specialty: Steamed wild Setouchi conger eel (蒸し穴子) nigiri — using exclusively wild-caught eel from the Seto Inland Sea, prepared by steaming rather than grilling to preserve the ingredient's delicate umami and give it a characteristically fluffy texture, finished with the house nitsume reduction.
bio.philosophy: Hisamune's guiding principle is to avoid diminishing ingredients' natural flavour while coaxing out their full umami through careful technique. He sources rice from the family's own paddies in Shisou City and uses only domestic, traceable ingredients, describing the goal as food that is 'safe and delicious' from children to the elderly.

anecdotes.text: Wild Setouchi anago now costs five to ten times more than imported varieties due to overfishing and coastal development, yet すし一 refuses to switch — the chef maintains that foreign eel from regions with inadequate sewage treatment poses food-safety risks he is unwilling to accept.
Hisamune Ryuichi is described by the restaurant itself as 'a very serious man' — a non-smoker, non-gambler who has given up alcohol on doctor's orders, dedicating himself entirely to the kitchen.
The restaurant was featured in the Michelin Guide Hyogo Prefecture edition and on Kansai TV's popular dining show 'Yoi Don', bringing wider attention to Himeji's conger-eel tradition.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information on cross-contamination protocols has been published. The kitchen handles wheat-based shoyu, tare, and potentially tempura batter — standard risk for a traditional sushi-kaiseki kitchen.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard Japanese shoyu (wheat-containing) is used for nigiri and dipping; the house-made nitsume (anago glaze) is soy-sauce based and therefore also contains wheat. No tamari or GF shoyu option is advertised.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Fish-based dashi underpins the kaiseki cooking. Conger eel, sea bream, octopus, and other marine proteins are handled throughout the kitchen with no plant-only preparation zone documented.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The restaurant states it aims for 'safe and reliable meals' for all ages and uses domestic traceable ingredients, but publishes no formal allergen matrix or allergy protocol. Same-day phone reservations are accepted, making it possible to communicate needs in advance.

positives.text: Exclusive use of wild-caught Seto Inland Sea conger eel (no farmed or imported substitutes) and rice from the owner's own paddies — traceability of key ingredients is unusually strong for a restaurant of this type.

caveats: Hours are irregular; closed days include obon and year-end periods but are unannounced — confirm by phone before visiting. The Tabelog URL uses the English-language subdomain; the Japanese version is at tabelog.com/hyogo/. Michelin recognition cited from secondary sources (Favy, Hitosara); the specific edition year was not confirmed directly from a Michelin source.

address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市駅前町188-1 グランフェスタ 5番街 B1F
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 姫路市駅前町188 returned 34.827621, 134.690842 — approximately 0.62 km from target center, well within 1.15 km radius.
neighborhood: Himeji Station (姫路駅) underground shopping arcade Gran Festa
hours_raw: 10:00〜20:00（グランフェスタの営業スケジュールに準ずる）
closed_days: Follows Gran Festa building schedule; no fixed weekly closing day confirmed
seats_text: Counter seating in a food court setting; no private tables
cultural_comfort_note: Ticket-vending-machine ordering, counter service, all Japanese signage; staff interaction is minimal but the format is intuitive once you understand the machine.
vegan_detail: Every menu item depends on animal products. The signature akashi-yaki batter contains egg. The dashi dipping broth is made from four fish-based stocks — sand eel, bonito, skipjack tuna, and mackerel. The ika-yaki is grilled squid. No plant-based substitutions are available at this counter.
gf_detail: Takoyaki and akashi-yaki batter is made with wheat flour; this is a core, non-substitutable ingredient. The dashi may also contain soy sauce (wheat). Other items on the menu — soba-yaki (wheat noodles), omurice — also contain gluten. There are no gluten-free menu options and no dedicated GF preparation area. Not safe for celiac or wheat-allergy diners.

bio.background: Originally founded in 1959 under the name タコピア (Takopia) in the basement of the Himeji Station building, the shop spent six decades as one of Himeji's most beloved soul-food counters. Longtime fixture Matsumoto Emiko — affectionately known as a local 'obachan' personality — worked there for decades and became inseparable from the shop's identity. After the original operator retired following a bereavement in late 2019, Himeji's Maneki Foods (まねき食品株式会社), a company already famous across the region for 'eki-soba' railway noodles, took over the recipe and tradition. They relaunched the counter in March 2020 as タコピィ (Takopii) — essentially the same name with one syllable changed — in the Gran Festa underground arcade where it still operates today.
bio.origin: Himeji, Hyogo Prefecture; originally 1959 in the old Himeji Station basement, current location since 2020
bio.specialty: Akashi-yaki-style takoyaki: fluffy balls of yamato-yam-enriched batter around octopus, first brushed with Worcestershire-style sauce and then dipped into a four-stock dashi — the combination eating style that the shop's customers invented organically and that has since become 'Himeji style'
bio.philosophy: Preserve the 60-year-old taste exactly as it was, down to the ticket vending machine format and the four-fish dashi blend that is never made from commercial stock concentrate.
roles: Operated by Maneki Foods (まねき食品株式会社)
Head cook position requires a minimum three months of dedicated training

anecdotes.text: The famous double-dip eating style — sauce first, then dashi — was not invented by the shop. The founder placed sauce bottles on tables so customers could choose between sauce or broth. Eventually some patrons started doing both, and that habit slowly became the house norm.

At peak season the counter sells over 1,500 portions a day, with queues forming outside the arcade entrance. The head-cook role is physically demanding enough that repeated hand movements reportedly cause tendonitis or permanent changes to finger shape.

When Maneki Foods took over, one of the first challenges was sourcing the same model of ticket-vending machine that dispenses plastic meal cards — the supplier was difficult to find, but staff insisted on keeping the format identical to what longtime regulars expected.

gf_cross_contamination.text: The kitchen is a compact open counter producing wheat-flour takoyaki continuously all day. There is no separation of gluten-containing and gluten-free cooking surfaces; cross-contact is certain.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No ingredient list is published, but standard Japanese takoyaki batter and dashi tsuyu both typically incorporate wheat-containing soy sauce. The shop has not disclosed a wheat-free soy sauce substitute.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The dashi broth is made from four fish stocks (sand eel, bonito, skipjack tuna, mackerel) and is present on every table and in every order. There is no vegan dashi option and no way to avoid fish contact.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergen menu, staff allergy protocol, or allergy inquiry option has been noted in any review or official source. The counter operates a high-volume self-service ticket machine format with limited staff interaction.

positives.text: Extremely straightforward menu — only a handful of items — so what you see is what you get; no hidden complex sauces beyond the house dashi and Worcestershire-style sauce.

caveats: Hours follow Gran Festa building schedule and may vary on holidays or building maintenance days — confirm before visiting. Cash only, ticket-vending-machine ordering. Price shown as ¥480-550 for 10 pieces depending on source and date; verify current price on arrival. The shop has undergone one rebrand (Takopia → Takopii) in 2020 and the Tabelog listing still carries the old name TACCO PIRE in parentheses — all refer to the same counter.

name_ja: 炭焼あなご やま義
cuisine: Anago (conger eel) — charcoal-grilled anago, anago-meshi (eel rice bowl), anago sushi, anago-wappa set
address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市駅前町301
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI AddressSearch on 兵庫県姫路市駅前町３０１番地; coordinates lat 34.829098 / lng 134.692093, approximately 440 m from target center — well within 1.15 km radius.
neighborhood: 駅前町 / 山陽姫路駅近く (Omizo-dori shopping arcade, 3-minute walk from JR/Sanyo Himeji Station)
seats_text: 14 seats total; small counter-and-table shop inside Omizo-dori shopping arcade; solo diners welcome
cultural_comfort_note: A beloved local institution in a covered shotengai; staff are welcoming but the restaurant is very small and popular, so basic Japanese communication helps — no English menu confirmed.
gf_detail: All core dishes use standard Japanese soy sauce (wheat-containing) and a proprietary tare sauce brushed repeatedly on the eel during grilling — wheat exposure is certain in the sauce. Dashi broth served alongside is fish-based but likely prepared with standard (wheat) soy sauce. Anago tempura on the menu is battered (wheat). No gluten-free claims or adaptations are mentioned anywhere. Celiacs should not eat here without detailed staff consultation, and cross-contamination in a 14-seat open kitchen is a real risk.
vegan_detail: Entirely an eel/fish specialist. Every dish centres on conger eel (anago). Dashi broth is fish-based. There are no plant-based or vegan options.

bio.background: Born 1985 in Hyogo Prefecture. Began cooking at an Italian restaurant in Himeji, gaining experience across Italian, pastry, and Japanese cuisine before joining Yamayoshi in 2018.
bio.origin: Claimed locally as Himeji's first anago-meshi (conger eel rice bowl) specialty restaurant, operating inside the Omizo-dori covered shopping arcade a short walk from Himeji Station. The shop has built a following over roughly twelve years and is frequently featured on regional TV.
bio.specialty: Charcoal-grilled conger eel (炭焼あなご) — each eel grilled individually over charcoal, brushed 2–4 times with a house tare built up from a long-standing stock, then steamed to finish. Signature anago-wappa presents three preparations: sweetly-simmered tsukudani, soft-steamed, and charcoal-grilled, served over tea-seasoned rice with dashi soup and pickles.
bio.philosophy: Uncompromising sourcing — only fresh raw eel (rare among Himeji shops) — and the belief that exceptional ingredients, treated simply, bring smiles to diners.

anecdotes.text: The proprietor has stated: 'I want people to taste the true deliciousness of anago, so I spare no compromise in selecting premium rice, eel, and all other ingredients.' (本来のあなごの美味しさを食べてもらいたい気持ちで、妥協なく厳選されたお米やあなごを初めとする素材にこだわって作っています)

The anago-wappa is designed to be eaten three ways: plain with the house tare, with wasabi for contrast, and finally as a tea-over-rice (ochazuke) with the dashi soup poured over the remains — a tasting ritual unique to the restaurant.

gf_cross_contamination.text: 14-seat open kitchen with no separation between preparation areas; no information about dedicated fryers or wheat-free cooking zones.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: House tare — applied repeatedly to eel during charcoal grilling — is made with standard Japanese soy sauce, which contains wheat. Dashi soup served alongside also likely uses wheat-containing soy sauce. No tamari or wheat-free soy sauce alternative mentioned.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All dishes are fish/eel-based; the kitchen has no plant-based cooking track and vegan cross-contact is unavoidable.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy handling information is published. Given the tiny size (14 seats) and single-specialty format, detailed allergy inquiries would need to be made by phone in Japanese before visiting.

positives.text: Single-ingredient specialty kitchen (conger eel only) means no cross-contact from unrelated proteins or cuisines found in multi-concept restaurants.

caveats: Hours listed as 無休 (no holidays) on all sources checked, but a restaurant of this size may close for owner holidays without notice — calling ahead on weekdays is advisable. Reservation policy conflicted slightly across sources (Tabelog: 予約不可; official site implies calling is possible for advance orders); walk-in is the safe assumption. Chef biography details from Hitosara may reflect a past employee rather than the current owner.

cuisine: French bistro with Italian influences; seasonal course menus
address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市神屋町6-25
geocode_note: GSI geocode returned lat 34.827831, lng 134.699615 for 兵庫県姫路市神屋町六丁目２５番地. Approximately 0.7km from target center (34.8331, 134.6921) — well within the 1.15km radius.
neighborhood: 神屋町 (Kamiyacho), ~12 min walk north of Himeji Station near Aclie Himeji
seats_text: Around 10 seats; a mix of counter and table seating in an intimate, adult-only space (no children under 12).
hours_raw: Mon/Tue/Thu-Sun 11:30-14:30 (L.O. 13:00), 18:00-22:00 (L.O. 20:00); Wed closed. Dinner availability may vary by date — call ahead.
cultural_comfort_note: Phone reservation in Japanese is required; no English-language interface or international outreach found, though the cuisine style is Western.
vegan_detail: Classic French bistro cooking centered on meat, fish, butter, and cream — no vegan menu or options noted anywhere. Hidden animal products are the norm in French sauces (stocks, butter, cream). Inquiry in Japanese before booking is essential; customization is unlikely in a 10-seat course-only format.
gf_detail: No gluten-free information found. French bistro cooking routinely uses wheat flour in roux, pan sauces, bread courses, and pasta. With only ~10 seats and set course menus, cross-contamination risk is significant and celiac safety cannot be assumed. Inquire in Japanese before booking — confirmation of safe preparation is not guaranteed.

bio.background: Owner-chef Uchida is originally from Tokyo and trained in French cuisine at multiple establishments before relocating to Himeji following his marriage. He pursued his longstanding goal of running his own restaurant in his wife's hometown, opening Bistro Usuke on July 27, 2012. After nearly a decade at the original location in 北平野, the restaurant relocated in June 2021 to its current intimate space in 神屋町, near Aclie Himeji.
bio.specialty: Seasonal French-Italian course meals built around fresh, locally sourced ingredients — fish preparations such as flounder carpaccio and fresh oysters appear in documented menus.
bio.philosophy: The kitchen's guiding belief is that pursuing genuine deliciousness ultimately leads back to seasonal, fresh ingredients. Uchida sources from trusted local suppliers and adapts the menu continuously to what is best at the market rather than fixing a permanent carte.

anecdotes.text: When searching for the new premises, the chef found the process difficult but formed valuable connections along the way. The distinctive animal-motif cutlery rests from the original location were carried over to the new space as a continuing tradition.

caveats: No dietary safety information (gluten-free, vegan, allergen handling) was found on any source. This is a course-only French bistro with cash-only payment, strongly recommended reservations, and a children-under-12 restriction. Dinner service availability varies — always call ahead. Tabelog score is based on very few reviews (3–4).

cuisine: 日本料理・割烹・居酒屋
address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市本町174 アネスティ七番館1階
geocode_note: GSI AddressSearch on 兵庫県姫路市本町174 returned [134.688705, 34.83305]. Approximately 300m from target center — well within the 1.15km radius.
neighborhood: 本町エリア（姫路城南側）
seats_text: 3つの4人ボックス席（口コミより）。個室なし。全席喫煙可。
cultural_comfort_note: 小さな地元向け割烹居酒屋で、英語メニューや外国語対応の記載は一切なく、日本語のみの環境。
vegan_detail: 鰹節・昆布だしを基本とする伝統的な和食・刺身専門店。メニューには醤油ベースの調理（水ナスしそしょうが正油など）や海産物が中心で、植物性対応の記載・実績はなし。だし・醤油を除いた注文は現実的に難しい。
gf_detail: 刺身自体は小麦不使用だが、付け合わせや煮物には通常の醤油（小麦含有）を使用。天ぷらや揚げ物メニューがある場合は小麦粉を使用。専用フライヤーの有無・グルテン対応の記録は皆無。セリアックの方には確認が必要で、対応できる保証はない。

bio.specialty: 新鮮な刺身・活魚料理（活アジ造り、紀の川盛など）

anecdotes.text: かつて訪れたビジネス客が「フレッシュで美味しい刺身、牡蠣、珍しい魚（ぬれそれ）、踊るタコ、〆のラーメンまで全て最高」と評した。

gf_cross_contamination.text: グルテン交差汚染に関する情報は一切公開されていない。刺身中心の業態だが、厨房での小麦使用状況は不明。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: 醤油ベースの調理が複数メニューに使われており（水ナスしそしょうが正油など）、通常の小麦入り醤油が使用されていると考えられる。

vegan_cross_contact.text: 鰹節・魚介だしを使用した伝統的な日本料理店。植物性への配慮・切り替えに関する記録はなし。

staff_allergy_handling.text: アレルギー対応・グルテンフリー対応についての記載は食べログ・ヒトサラ・エキテンいずれにも見当たらない。訪問前に電話（079-223-1129）で直接確認を推奨。

positives.text: 刺身そのものは基本的に無添加・小麦不使用で提供されることが多く、醤油を避ければ選べる品がある可能性はある。

caveats: 閉店時間が公開されておらず、hours_weekの終了時刻は不明（「?」）。口コミ数が少なく（食べログ29件、TripAdvisor 2件）、最新口コミは数年前のもの。営業中と判断できるが、訪問前の電話確認を強く推奨。公式サイトなし。全席喫煙可のため注意。

cuisine: マーラータン（麻辣湯）・中華
neighborhood: 坂元町・国道2号沿い（姫路城南西）
address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市坂元町75 高田ビル1F
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 姫路市坂元町75. Approximately 600m from target center (34.8331, 134.6921), well within 1.15km radius.
hours_raw: 11:00〜22:00（ラストオーダー21:30）、無休（定休日なし）
closed_days: 定休日なし（要確認：新規開店のため変更の可能性あり）
seats_text: 43席（2人掛けテーブル×4、4人掛けテーブル×2、カウンター席あり）
cultural_comfort_note: 中国人オーナー・スタッフによる運営で、日本語対応は可能だが英語メニューや英語スタッフは期待しにくい。中国語堪能なスタッフも在籍。
vegan_detail: 具材は野菜・きのこ類を含む約60種類から選択可能だが、ベーススープは牛骨出汁（約20種類の漢方薬味使用）のため完全ビーガンには対応していない。トマト麻辣湯や汁なし麻辣拌も提供するが、動物性出汁不使用かは未確認。ビーガン対応を希望する場合は事前に直接確認が必要。
gf_detail: 麻辣湯スープには牛骨ベースと中国醤油・調味料が使用されている可能性が高く、醤油には小麦が含まれることが一般的。麺類は小麦麺が基本（グルテンフリー麺の有無は未確認）。グルテンフリー対応・専用調理環境についての公式情報なし。セリアック・重度小麦アレルギーの方は来店前に直接確認が必須。

bio.background: 中国・威海市で10年にわたり本場の麻辣湯修行を積んだシェフが手がける、姫路初出店の本格麻辣湯専門店。2026年4月にオープン。白鷺橋そば、姫路城の埋門跡向かいという歴史的なロケーションに位置する。
bio.specialty: 牛骨スープに約20種類の中国産漢方薬味を加えた麻辣湯。四川山椒（花椒）の清涼感ある痺れと唐辛子の辛みが調和した本場の味。約60種類の具材からセルフで選ぶスタイル。
bio.philosophy: 本場10年の修行で培った本格的なシビ辛体験を、自分好みにカスタマイズできる形で提供する。

anecdotes.text: オープン前から行列ができるほどの注目を集め、プレオープン時には半額クーポンを提供。中国人スタッフも在籍し、本場の雰囲気を醸し出している。

gf_cross_contamination.text: セルフ式でお客が具材をトレーに盛るスタイルのため、小麦含有食品（小麦麺・揚げパンなど）との交差汚染リスクが考えられる。専用グルテンフリー調理環境の情報なし。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: 中国式の麻辣湯スープには醤油・豆板醤等の調味料が使用される可能性が高く、これらには通常小麦が含まれる。スープの具体的な原材料リストは公開されていない。

vegan_cross_contact.text: ベーススープは牛骨出汁であるため、同じ鍋・調理器具で野菜具材を調理する場合、ビーガン非対応となる。

staff_allergy_handling.text: アレルギー対応・グルテンフリー対応に関する公式情報は一切公開されていない。来店前に電話（080-5301-5588）またはX（@umaimaratan）で確認することを強く推奨する。

positives.text: 約60種類の具材から自分で選べるセルフスタイルのため、野菜・きのこ類のみを選択することはできる。辛さは5段階から調整可能。

caveats: 2026年4月開店の新規店舗のため、営業時間・定休日・支払い方法は変更される可能性あり。Tabelog情報では現金のみ（カード・電子マネー不可）。グルテンフリー・ビーガン対応に関する公式情報は一切なく、来店前の直接確認を強く推奨。

address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市塩町173 レオビル1F
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 兵庫県姫路市塩町173; 480m from target center — within 1.15km radius.
neighborhood: 塩町 (Shiocho)
seats_text: 31 seats: counter (9), one horigotatsu private room (2-6), two table private rooms (2-8)
cultural_comfort_note: A small hidden kappo with counter seating and traditional private rooms; Japanese-speaking environment with no English menu or signage evident, though the chef is noted as personable and approachable.
vegan_detail: No vegan options identified. The menu is built around seafood — sashimi, conger eel, fugu, oysters — with standard fish-based dashi throughout. No plant-based or vegan-friendly course is offered or advertised.
gf_detail: No gluten-free information is disclosed anywhere. Standard kappo: wheat soy sauce is almost certainly used in grilled and simmered dishes; tempura batter and breaded items (wagyu katsu, oyster fry) contain wheat. Fish dashi is standard. Dedicated fryer status unknown. Guests with celiac disease should call ahead and inquire carefully — accommodation is not guaranteed.

bio.background: A small hidden kappo (隠れ家割烹) tucked into the Leo Building on Shiocho, a short walk from Himeji Station. The chef selects seasonal ingredients daily and tailors the meal to guests' preferences, offering everything from sake-pairing small plates to full kaiseki courses.
bio.specialty: Conger eel (穴子/anago) — served both in salt-grilled and white-grilled preparations — alongside fresh sashimi platters and seasonal seafood from Seto Inland Sea waters.
bio.philosophy: Seasonal ingredients, honest conversation across the counter, and flexible hospitality for couples, families, and business diners alike.

anecdotes.text: Reviewers note the chef is friendly and willing to prepare off-menu items on request, reflecting the counter-kappo ethos of cooking to each guest's mood.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Fried items — squid tempura, fugu fry, oyster fry, wagyu katsu — are all listed on the menu, making shared fryer cross-contamination a realistic concern. No dedicated fryer information is disclosed.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Wheat-based soy sauce is standard in kappo cuisine and is expected in grilled (yakimono) and simmered dishes. No tamari or gluten-free soy sauce alternative is mentioned.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Fish dashi (likely bonito/katsuo or kombu-bonito blend) is standard in Japanese kappo; no plant-based dashi option is mentioned. Virtually all dishes will contain animal products.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy policy or staff allergy-handling procedures are published. The chef is known to accommodate special requests when informed in advance — contact by phone (079-288-1302) before visiting.

caveats: Hours discrepancy: Tabelog and English Tabelog list opening at 17:30; Ekiten lists 17:00. Tabelog (the more authoritative source) is used. No official website exists; all data sourced from third-party listing platforms. Chef name is not publicly disclosed on any platform.

address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市白銀町78 野中ビル1F
neighborhood: 白銀町 (Shirogane-cho)
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 兵庫県姫路市白銀町78番地 returned [134.690399, 34.831165]. Approximately 264m from the target center (34.8331, 134.6921), well within the 1.15km radius.
seats_text: Around 6–8 seats at a single L-shaped counter surrounding the oden pot; solo-operated
hours_raw: 17:30～22:00、日曜日・祝日定休、8月休業あり
closed_days: Sundays, holidays; closed entirely in August
cultural_comfort_note: Tiny counter with a regular local crowd; no English signage or menu, and the solo proprietress runs everything alone — basic Japanese helps a lot.
vegan_detail: Traditional oden broth is dashi-based — almost certainly made with konbu and katsuo (bonito), making it non-vegan. Himeji oden is served with ginger soy sauce as a condiment, which adds fish-adjacent dashi flavour to the broth. Tsumire (fish-paste balls) and other fish-derived ingredients are mainstays of the 30-item pot. No plant-based or vegan options identified in any source.
gf_detail: Himeji oden uses standard Japanese soy sauce (含む小麦 — contains wheat) both in the broth and as the table condiment (ginger soy sauce). A house condiment called "karazou" (唐三), a fermented chili-soy paste, also contains wheat via soy sauce. Tsumire fish balls may include wheat-based binders. No gluten-free accommodation, dedicated equipment, or allergy awareness noted in any source. Not safe for celiacs.

bio.background: いち佳 occupies a low-key spot in a back alley of Himeji's 白銀町 drinking district, inside the Nonaka Building — a location that previously housed another oden counter called Nakamoto. The current proprietress, a woman reportedly with prior experience at a Himeji hotel, took it on and built a tight circle of regulars who fill the roughly six counter seats on most evenings.
bio.specialty: Himeji-style oden: about 30 rotating ingredients simmered in a soy-flavoured kombu dashi and eaten with ginger soy sauce — the regional hallmark. Yuba, tsumire, ginkgo nuts, roasted spring onions, and lotus root appear regularly.
bio.philosophy: A "empty the pot" ethos: the proprietress replenishes ingredients throughout the evening so the pot is always fresh, and she pairs the food with local Hyogo sake such as Naruto Tai and Toibijin.

anecdotes.text: A blogger notes that the spot occupies the former address of a predecessor oden shop called Nakamoto, and that the current proprietress seems to have inherited some of its long-standing regulars.
A Tabelog reviewer writes that the counter is consistently full of regulars and that reservations are essential despite the shop accepting walk-ins in principle.

gf_cross_contamination.text: All-in-one communal oden pot with no separation between ingredients; wheat-containing items such as tsumire (which may contain binders) and soy-sauce-marinated components share the same broth.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard Japanese soy sauce (wheat-containing) is used both in the oden broth and in the Himeji-style ginger soy sauce condiment served at the table.
The house condiment 'karazou' (唐三) is described as a fermented chili-soy paste, meaning it also contains wheat via soy sauce.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Bonito-based dashi is the foundation of the oden broth; fish-paste tsumire and other animal-derived ingredients share the pot with any vegetable items.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy or dietary accommodation information was found in any source. The restaurant operates as a solo-run counter with no documented awareness of gluten or vegan requirements.

caveats: Hours (17:30 start) from the official Himeji Oden cooperative listing; a blog post noted an 18:00 opening — confirm by phone (079-224-2867). Seating count varies across sources (6 per Tabelog, 8 per other directories); treat as approximately 6–8. Cash-only inference: Tabelog notes cards not accepted, only e-money; treat as effectively cash. Closed August confirmed by cooperative listing but duration unclear. No closure signals found as of mid-2025 search results.

neighborhood: 駅前エリア（グランフェスタ地下街）
address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市駅前町188-1 グランフェスタ 6番街
geocode_note: GSI geocode returned [134.690842, 34.827621] for 姫路市駅前町188番地. Approximately 0.6 km from target center — well within the 1.15 km radius.
closed_days: 不定休（グランフェスタ商業施設の営業カレンダーに準ずる）
seats_text: 全26席（カウンター10席、テーブル16席）。最大30名まで対応可、個室は応相談。
cultural_comfort_note: Menu and signage are in Japanese only; no English-language materials noted, though the station-adjacent location means staff may have basic tourist experience.
vegan_detail: This is a shellfish and seafood izakaya with oysters as its core identity. Virtually all dishes center on oysters, clams, shrimp, scallops, and other marine life. A small number of meat dishes are listed for non-shellfish diners, but there are no plant-based offerings and dashi/broth in Japanese-style cooking here would almost certainly contain bonito or other fish stock. Vegans have no viable options.
gf_detail: The restaurant specializes in grilled, steamed, and raw shellfish — the core proteins are naturally gluten-free. However, karaage (fried oysters / カキフライ) are battered with wheat flour and feature prominently on the menu, including the popular lunch sets. Shoyu (wheat-based soy sauce) is standard in Japanese izakaya seasoning. No tamari or GF soy sauce is mentioned anywhere. Dashi sources are unspecified. No dedicated fryer information is available; fried items and grilled items likely share kitchen space. Celiac diners should call ahead and focus strictly on raw (生牡蠣) or simply steamed/grilled shellfish (浜焼き) with no sauces — but cross-contamination risk is unconfirmed and staff allergy protocols are not documented.

bio.background: 貝屋 マルホウは播州室津（兵庫県たつの市御津町）を拠点とする吉田水産の直営店として姫路駅前地下街グランフェスタ6番街に出店した貝専門の海鮮居酒屋。水産会社が直接運営することで、室津港から毎日仕入れる獲れたて・新鮮な牡蠣や貝類を産地直送価格で提供できる体制を持つ。
bio.specialty: 浜焼きスタイルで提供される播州室津産の牡蠣。テーブルで自ら焼く体験型の浜焼きセットと、90分食べ放題（11月〜5月）が看板。生牡蠣、蒸し牡蠣、カキフライ、アヒージョなど幅広い調理法で展開する。
bio.origin: 兵庫県姫路市（姫路駅直結グランフェスタ地下街）
bio.philosophy: 「貝に出会えてよかった」をキャッチコピーに掲げ、水産会社直営の強みを活かして産地から食卓まで最短距離で新鮮な貝を届けることを理念とする。生簀で活かしたまま仕入れ、注文を受けてから調理する鮮度重視のスタイル。

anecdotes.text: ランチの牡蠣定食（¥1,000）を注文するとInstagramフォローで蒸し牡蠣2個が無料サービスされる。「信じられない量」と地元ブロガーが驚くほどのコストパフォーマンスが話題。
姫路駅地下街という立地のため雨の日でもアクセス可能で、駅から徒歩1分という利便性がビジネスマンや旅行者に支持されている。

gf_cross_contamination.text: カキフライ（小麦粉パン粉衣）がメニューの主力であり、同じ厨房でグリル・生食・揚げ物が並行して提供される。専用フライヤーの有無は非公開。小麦を使用しない浜焼き・生牡蠣との調理動線上の分離も未確認。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: 醤油・たれ類の使用が想定されるが、グルテンフリー醤油（タマリ等）の提供は公式サイト・各種予約サイトいずれにも記載なし。焼き貝に添えるタレに小麦含有醤油が使われる可能性が高い。

vegan_cross_contact.text: 全メニューが海鮮ベース。植物性食材との分離調理の概念はこの業態では想定しにくく、ヴィーガン対応の実績・表記なし。

staff_allergy_handling.text: アレルギー対応・スタッフへの指示プロトコルについていずれの媒体にも記載なし。セリアック・重篤アレルギーの場合は来店前に電話（079-262-6325）で直接確認が必須。

positives.text: 生牡蠣・蒸し牡蠣・浜焼き（単品でソースなし）は本来グルテンフリーの調理法。水産会社直営で添加物を使わない素材重視のコンセプトは、シンプルに食べられる料理を頼む場合にはプラス材料。

caveats: Hours follow the GrandFesta shopping center calendar (irregular holidays). Seat count varies across sources (23 on Tabelog, 26 on HotPepper); 26 is the more recently confirmed figure. Oyster all-you-can-eat is seasonal (November–May). No allergen or GF protocol documentation found anywhere — celiac diners must call ahead.

name_ja: はりかい 姫路城店
cuisine: バル・立ち飲み（播磨灘の鮮魚・活魚）
address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市本町68 姫路公園にの屋敷
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 姫路市本町68 returned [134.687363, 34.835873]. Approximately 530m from target center (34.8331, 134.6921) — well within the 1.15km radius.
neighborhood: 本町68 にの屋敷（姫路公園内、大手前通り沿い）
seats_text: カウンター席とテラス席（屋外のみ。雨風の影響を受けやすい）。具体的な席数は非公開。
cultural_comfort_note: The owner speaks English and the shop provides a photo menu in English; it was specifically featured by Japan's Ministry of Agriculture as a model for welcoming international visitors at Himeji Castle.
vegan_detail: The menu is seafood-centric — grilled oysters, raw oysters, octopus rice, conger eel, fish and chips. No documented vegan dishes. Hidden fish dashi is extremely likely throughout. Visitors should inquire directly, though options are expected to be very limited.
gf_detail: A signature item is fish and chips (フィッシュアンドチップス), which is almost certainly wheat-battered. Standard Japanese soy sauce (wheat-containing) is standard in Japanese seafood preparations. No gluten-free menu, dedicated fryer, or allergen protocols are documented. Celiacs should ask carefully before ordering; dedicated GF safety cannot be assumed.

bio.background: The shop is operated by 有限会社播磨海洋牧場 (Harima Marine Ranch Co., Ltd.), an aquaculture and seafood company founded in 2001 by Akihiro Mukai. After years in fish wholesale and online seafood sales (launched 2004), the company opened this Himeji Castle-front storefront in 2011 to bring their catch directly to consumers and tourists.
bio.specialty: Seasonal fresh seafood from the Harima-nada (播磨灘) stretch of the Seto Inland Sea — especially their own-brand 播磨の白珠 (Harima White Pearl) virgin oysters, prized for low bitterness, plus grilled oysters, raw oysters, octopus rice balls, conger eel kabayaki, and fish and chips.
bio.philosophy: The company motto is 'challenge without fearing failure' (失敗を恐れずに挑戦する精神). Mukai-san aims to shorten the chain between sea and table, combining sustainable aquaculture with direct retail so visitors to the World Heritage castle can taste the same fish landed by Harima fishermen.

anecdotes.text: Japan's Ministry of Agriculture (近畿農政局) selected はりかい姫路城店 as a model case study for inbound tourism best practices — one of very few small food stalls in the Kinki region to receive this recognition.

TripAdvisor reviewers ranked it #1 of 67 bars and pubs in Himeji, with repeated praise for Mukai-san personally working the counter alone during busy periods while still managing to engage foreign visitors warmly.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Fish and chips is a signature menu item and almost certainly uses wheat batter; no dedicated fryer is documented. Cross-contamination from shared frying oil is plausible.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard Japanese soy sauce (containing wheat) is the baseline seasoning in a Japanese seafood bar. No tamari or GF soy sauce alternatives are documented at this establishment.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The kitchen handles fish, shellfish, eel, and octopus exclusively. No plant-only preparation area is documented; vegan cross-contact is highly probable.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The owner speaks English and is described as attentive and personally engaged with customers. While no formal allergy protocol is documented, the owner's language ability means direct communication about dietary needs is more feasible than at most casual seafood stalls.

positives.text: English-speaking owner makes it possible to have a real conversation about ingredients, which is significantly better than the norm for casual Japanese seafood bars.
English photo menu is available, reducing ordering uncertainty for foreign visitors.

caveats: Hours (10:00–17:00 daily) are daytime only and closures are irregular — confirm before visiting, especially on weekdays. The outdoor-only terrace is exposed to weather. No GF or vegan menu exists; celiac and vegan diners should treat this as an "ask" venue only and note that a wheat-battered fish and chips dish is a core menu item.

address_ja: 〒670-0927 兵庫県姫路市駅前町188-1 駅前地下街グランフェスタ5番街
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI AddressSearch on 駅前町188番地; result lat 34.827621 lng 134.690842 is approximately 618m from target center (34.8331, 134.6921), well within the 1.15km radius. The listing area description references ピオレ姫路 and 山陽姫路駅 but this shop is in グランフェスタ, the underground shopping street directly beneath JR Himeji Station — both form part of the same Himeji station complex.
neighborhood: 駅前地下街グランフェスタ (JR Himeji Station Underground)
seats_text: 28 seats (counter + tables); Tabelog lists 23, press release and media list 28
hours_raw: 11:00–15:00 (L.O. 14:30) as 喫茶ラヴリ; evening transforms to bistro bar YORU NO MARUMASA 16:00–23:00 (L.O. 22:30). Irregular holidays (不定休).
cultural_comfort_note: Underground kissaten catering to local commuters and shoppers; no English signage or staff language accommodation indicated, but the casual counter-seat format is welcoming to solo diners.
vegan_detail: The menu is built around meat-centric dishes: signature chicken katsu fried rice, karaage, ginger pork, omelette rice, and mapo tofu. No plant-based or dairy-free options are identified anywhere. Hidden animal products (dashi, lard, egg in fried batters) are highly probable throughout.
gf_detail: The kitchen fries breaded chicken cutlets and karaage in the same oil as everything else — no dedicated gluten-free fryer. Signature dishes are heavily wheat-dependent: breaded katsu, fried rice with standard soy sauce (wheat-containing), napolitan spaghetti, curry roux. No allergen menu or GF claims appear on any source. Not appropriate for celiac or serious wheat allergy.

bio.background: Operated by 株式会社ATE-LINK (MARUMASA Group), a restaurant operator active across the Harima area of Hyogo Prefecture. The concept draws lineage from 喫茶ラヴリ, a beloved mega-portion café originally established in Fukusaki Town, roughly 30 minutes north of Himeji, known for its faithful following among locals who prize value and volume over refinement.
bio.origin: Fukusaki Town, Hyogo Prefecture (original location); Himeji branch opened March 20, 2026
bio.specialty: チキンカツ焼き飯 — a thick house-breaded chicken cutlet laid over 400g of fried rice with housemade char siu, the whole plate tipping the scales above 1 kg. The challenge item 'ラヴリスペシャル' reaches approximately 2 kg.
bio.philosophy: Volume as a virtue: every item is made to order from scratch — sauces, dressings, batters all housemade — on the principle that generous portions and careful preparation are equally important. Reusable containers (¥50) are offered for those who cannot finish.
roles: operator: 株式会社ATE-LINK / MARUMASA Group
Instagram manager: 高橋恵美 (Takahashi Emi)

anecdotes.text: The Himeji branch doubles as a completely different restaurant after 3 pm: lighting drops, the menu pivots to roast beef, bruschetta, and mapo tofu, and the space reopens as YORU NO MARUMASA — a bistro bar sharing the same underground address.

When the Fukusaki original first opened under the name 喫茶ラヴリ, its 'bug-level' (バグ盛り) portions quickly made it a regional talking point; the Himeji outpost carries the same outsized ambition into the heart of the city.

gf_cross_contamination.text: The kitchen fries breaded chicken cutlets (panko-coated), karaage, and other wheat-battered items together. No source mentions a separate fryer or GF protocols.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard wheat-containing soy sauce is almost certainly used in fried rice, sauces, and marinades; no tamari or GF soy sauce alternative is mentioned in any source.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All dishes observed involve meat, egg, or dairy. The kitchen is not set up for plant-based preparation; cross-contact with animal products in every dish is essentially certain.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergen menu, allergy-handling protocol, or staff training for dietary restrictions is mentioned in any source. The restaurant does not market itself to diners with dietary needs.

caveats: Opened March 20, 2026 — a very new venue with limited review depth. Seat count differs across sources (Tabelog: 23; press release: 28). The listing area cites ピオレ姫路・山陽姫路駅直結 but the actual address is グランフェスタ5番街, the underground street beneath JR Himeji Station; geocode confirms it is within the target radius. Cash + QR code payment (Rakuten Pay, au PAY, Alipay) per Tabelog — not strictly cash-only. Independent flag is false: operated by 株式会社ATE-LINK / MARUMASA Group. Evening bistro mode (YORU NO MARUMASA, 16:00–23:00) is a distinct concept at the same address and is not reflected in the café hours above.

address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市駅前町229 1F
geocode_note: GSI address-search on 姫路市駅前町229 returned [134.690842, 34.827621]; within target box (lat 34.823–34.843, lng 134.679–134.705)
neighborhood: 駅前町 (Himeji Station north exit, 1-minute walk)
seats_text: 17 seats — 13 counter, 4 table
cultural_comfort_note: Compact counter-heavy ramen shop steps from Himeji Station; no English menu known, but ordering by pointing at Japanese menu is feasible.
vegan_detail: The broth is a clear chintan made from chicken and pork stock. No plant-based broth option is listed. Gyoza and karaage side dishes also contain animal products. The restaurant does not advertise any vegan menu items.
gf_detail: Standard wheat noodles are the core of every dish. The soup is seasoned with conventional soy sauce (wheat-containing) via a table-side ginger-soy condiment. No gluten-free noodle substitute or tamari option has been identified. Gyoza wrappers and karaage batter add further wheat exposure. This shop is not safe for celiac diners.

bio.background: 姫路タンメン opened in February 2017 a one-minute walk from JR Himeji Station's north exit, quickly earning a loyal local following with its vegetable-forward take on tanmen. The shop expanded with a second branch in Fukusaki (December 2019) and a third in Tohori (June 2022, closed January 2026), while the original Ekimae-cho counter remains the flagship.
bio.specialty: The house tanmen: 360 g of stir-fried vegetables in a clear chicken-and-pork chintan broth, served with table-side minced ginger, house ginger-soy sauce, and garlic oil — the restaurant's tagline is 野菜たりてますか？ (Are you getting enough vegetables?)
bio.philosophy: Volume and freshness first — every bowl packs a full day's worth of vegetables cooked at high heat in a wok to preserve crunch before being ladled into the soup.

anecdotes.text: When a Fukusaki branch opened in December 2019 with 80 seats and triple the menu items of the original, the Ekimae-cho flagship stayed small at 17 seats, preserving the counter-ramen intimacy.
In July 2025 the shop closed for about two weeks for interior air-conditioning work — a rare disruption for a shop that otherwise runs nearly every day of the year.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Wheat noodles are boiled in shared water for every bowl; karaage (fried chicken) and gyoza are also on the menu, adding further wheat exposure throughout the kitchen.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The signature table-side condiment is a house-made ginger soy sauce; standard Japanese soy sauce contains wheat. No tamari or wheat-free soy sauce alternative has been mentioned in any source.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Broth is chicken-and-pork chintan; animal stock is present in every soup bowl. There is no plant-based broth option on the menu.

caveats: Hours listed as 11:00–24:00 appear consistently across Tabelog, Gnavi, and Retty but last-order time varies by source (23:30 on some). Closed days are irregular; January 1 is the only confirmed annual closure. The shop underwent a ~2-week AC-repair closure in July–August 2025 (main Ekimae-cho location) and has since resumed normal service. The Tohori branch closed permanently January 24, 2026 — this record covers only the original Ekimae-cho shop.

name_ja: オーガニックダイニングvegeta
cuisine: オーガニック創作料理
address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市紺屋町71
geocode_note: GSI AddressSearch on 兵庫県姫路市紺屋町71 returned lng 134.692169 / lat 34.830639, approximately 273 m south of the target centre — within the 1.15 km radius. TripAdvisor shows different coordinates (34.81409, 134.6845) which appear erroneous and were disregarded.
neighborhood: 山陽姫路駅周辺 / おみぞ筋商店街
hours_raw: 月～木・日・祝日・祝前日 11:00～21:00、金・土 11:00～22:00（定休日：不定休）。なお、開店当初の情報では昼 11:00-14:30・夜 17:00-20:45 の二部制だったが、HotPepper掲載の現行情報では通し営業に変更されている模様。
seats_text: カウンター4席（1階）＋テーブル席18席（2階）。個室あり。
cultural_comfort_note: No English signage or menu confirmed; staff may speak limited English, but one traveller review in English suggests the visit was manageable.
vegan_detail: The menu is omnivore-oriented and prominently features meat dishes (pork kimchi, chicken karaage, hambagu steak). Organic vegetable sides and rice dishes may be ordered, and the lunch sets include organic salads and mixed-grain rice, but no dedicated vegan menu exists. Hidden dashi (bonito/katsuo) in miso soup and sauces is likely — ask before ordering.
gf_detail: No dedicated gluten-free menu or documented allergen protocols. The restaurant prominently uses JAS-certified organic rice and organic vegetables, which are inherently GF, but standard Japanese cooking relies on wheat-containing soy sauce (shoyu) in marinades, sauces, and dressings. One TripAdvisor reviewer (July 2024) noted staff were "lovely and accommodating" for a gluten-free diner, but no specifics about substitutions or cross-contamination controls were described. Celiac diners should call ahead and ask specifically about shoyu use in each dish.

bio.background: Owner Shimotada Masayuki brought 17 years of food-service industry experience when he opened Organic Dining Vegeta in September 2023 along Himeji's Omizo shopping arcade (おみぞ筋商店街). He identified a gap in the local market as health consciousness and dietary awareness among diners began to shift.
bio.specialty: Creative lunch sets built around seasonal organic vegetables — such as a tofu-hijiki burger plate or salmon-and-mushroom tofu set — served with organic salad, miso soup, and a choice of brown rice or multi-grain rice.
bio.philosophy: Vegetables are the main event. Shimotada sources directly from Migita Farm in Tatsuno City (a circular-agriculture operation) and a network of organic specialists nationwide, aiming to put the best seasonal produce on the plate and refine the menu continuously in response to customer feedback.

anecdotes.text: The opening was covered by Himeji Keizai Shimbun one month after launch, highlighting that Shimotada chose the Omizo arcade location because he saw a clear need for organic-vegetable-led cuisine in central Himeji.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information about dedicated fryer or separate preparation areas for gluten-free orders. The kitchen serves both wheat-containing dishes (chicken karaage uses breading, hambagu steak may use breadcrumbs) and the organic vegetable dishes in the same space.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard Japanese soy sauce (containing wheat) is almost certainly used in marinades, dressings, and cooked dishes. No tamari or GF shoyu alternative is documented on any source reviewed.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Miso soup is served with lunch sets; dashi base (likely bonito/katsuo) is undisclosed. Meat dishes such as pork kimchi and chicken karaage are cooked in the same kitchen.

staff_allergy_handling.text: One TripAdvisor reviewer from July 2024 described staff as 'lovely and accommodating' for a gluten-free diner and called the food 'well presented', but no specifics about protocols, substitutions, or dedicated handling were given.

positives.text: All rice uses JAS-certified pesticide-free organic cultivation (milky queen or mixed grain), which is inherently gluten-free. Organic vegetable sides and salads form the core of the menu.
Ingredients are sourced direct from Migita Farm (circular agriculture, Tatsuno City) and vetted organic suppliers — likely lower additive load than conventional restaurants.

caveats: Tabelog does not currently list this restaurant among Himeji organic/café results, so no Tabelog URL could be confirmed. Hours may have evolved since the September 2023 opening; HotPepper shows continuous-service hours while the launch article described a split lunch/dinner format — verify directly before visiting. Cash-only status inferred from HotPepper's クレジットカード利用不可 notation but should be confirmed. No official website or social media account was located.

address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市駅前町363-1 新フェスタビル 1F（姫路のれん街内）
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI AddressSearch on 姫路市駅前町363-1. Result at 34.827621, 134.690842 — approximately 0.62 km from target center, well within the 1.15 km radius.
neighborhood: 駅前町・JR姫路駅南口徒歩1分
seats_text: 48席（カウンター席・カップルシート・テーブル席）。Hotpepperでは62席と記載あり。
closed_days: 1月1日のみ定休（年間約4回臨時休業あり）
cultural_comfort_note: Casual izakaya-style sushi bar in a lively station-front arcade; English menu not confirmed, but counter seating lets you point at dishes — basic Japanese or a phone translation app is helpful.
vegan_detail: The menu is built entirely around seafood — sushi, sashimi, grilled shellfish, fried fish. Fish-based dashi underlies miso soup served with most set meals. No plant-only dishes were identified; vegan eating here is not practicable.
gf_detail: Standard wheat-based soy sauce (tamari is not listed) is used across the sushi and sashimi menu. Fried items (fried oysters, fried squid tentacles, fried octopus, croquettes, karaage) are on the menu, implying a shared fryer with wheat flour. Miso soup with sets contains dashi. No gluten-free menu, no allergen declarations published. A coeliac diner would need to confirm tamari availability and cross-contamination policy by phone (079-222-0158) before visiting — do not assume safety.

bio.background: Opened in September 2017 inside Himeji Norengai, a covered izakaya alley occupying the first floor of the Shin Festa Building one minute from JR Himeji Station's south exit. The concept was conceived as a place where high-quality Edomae-style sushi — normally associated with formal counter dining — could be enjoyed casually alongside sake in a lively, affordable neighborhood setting.
bio.specialty: Signature pieces include 'Maguro Rossini' (seared fatty tuna with a rich topping inspired by the classic French preparation) and 'Noméru Shizen Shirozake' (dubbed 'drinkable natural white salmon'), a piece so silky and delicate diners joke they could drink it. The kitchen also offers hamayaki — table-top grilling of fresh shellfish — giving the menu a dual identity as both sushi bar and seafood izakaya.
bio.philosophy: Uncompromising freshness: fish is sourced daily from across Japan, and the kitchen insists on preparations unavailable elsewhere. The pairing of carefully chosen regional sake with creative sushi is central to the experience.

anecdotes.text: The restaurant's article introduction calls it a place where 'smiles naturally overflow' — crediting the combination of quality sushi and curated sake as the cause.
The 'Maguro Rossini' and 'drinkable natural white salmon' are flagged as dishes 'unavailable anywhere else', reflecting the kitchen's drive toward invention within the Edomae tradition.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Fried items (fried oysters, fried squid tentacles, fried octopus, croquettes, anko karaage, beef menchi katsu) appear on the menu. A dedicated fryer separate from wheat-flour batters is not confirmed; shared-fryer cross-contamination must be assumed absent explicit confirmation.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard wheat-containing soy sauce is the default seasoning for sushi and sashimi. No tamari or gluten-free soy sauce alternative is listed anywhere on the menu or official sources.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Fish-based dashi is used in miso soup served alongside set meals. All menu sections feature seafood; no plant-only dishes identified. Cross-contact with fish products throughout the kitchen is unavoidable.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No published allergen declarations or allergy-handling policy found across Tabelog, Hotpepper, or the Festa Building shop guide. Diners with serious allergies should call 079-222-0158 before visiting.

positives.text: Fresh fish sourced daily from across Japan, prepared to order at the counter — individual pieces rather than pre-made rolls reduces some risk of hidden sauces, though soy sauce use remains standard.

caveats: Seat count discrepancy: Tabelog lists 48, Hotpepper lists 62 — 48 used as primary. Hours discrepancy: Tabelog/Gnavi cite 23:00 close, Hotpepper/budou-chan cite midnight — 23:00 used as the conservative figure. The official domain (himeji-noren.jp/uozushi/) returned a TLS error and could not be fetched; Festa Building shop guide and Hotpepper used as secondary official sources. No chef name or owner identity found in any public source.

address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市白銀町107 白銀ビル1F
geocode_note: GSI address-search on 兵庫県姫路市白銀町107 returned [134.690048, 34.830044]. Within target box (34.823–34.843, 134.679–134.705). Approx 384m from target center — well within 1.15km.
neighborhood: 白銀町 (Hakugin-cho), 山陽姫路駅から徒歩2分
cuisine: Himeji oden (姫路おでん) — regional oden eaten with ginger soy sauce
vegan_detail: Dashi is made from Rishiri kelp and bonito flakes (katsuo), making the broth non-vegan. The ginger soy sauce dipping condiment is conventional wheat-based shoyu blended with broth. Oden ingredients include fish cakes, octopus, beef tendon, and other animal products throughout the menu. No plant-only options are indicated.
gf_detail: The signature ginger soy sauce dipping condiment is conventional Japanese shoyu, which contains wheat. Dashi uses bonito (safe) and kelp (safe), but the soy sauce applied at the table is wheat-bearing. Fish cakes and processed oden ingredients may also contain wheat starch as binders. No gluten-free options or tamari substitution are mentioned anywhere. Not suitable for celiac diners without direct confirmation from the restaurant.
seats_text: 17席（L字カウンター中心、4〜5名テーブル1卓）
cultural_comfort_note: Counter-heavy small oden bar in a traditional drinking district; no English menu or signage noted, and staff likely communicate primarily in Japanese.

bio.background: A second-generation oden specialist in Himeji's Hakugin-cho drinking district, operating from the ground floor of a small neighbourhood building a 2-minute walk from Sanyo Himeji Station. The current proprietress, Hitomi Ohashi, took over the shop from her mother-in-law and has continued its dedication to a single dish: Himeji-style oden.
bio.origin: Opened approximately 2005; the shop's roots go back further through the founding generation.
bio.specialty: Himeji oden — around 25 varieties of seasonal ingredients simmered in a delicate kelp-and-bonito dashi and eaten with a house ginger soy sauce made by blending shoyu with cooking sake and a ladleful of the warm broth.
bio.philosophy: Quality over quantity: Ohashi-san sources every ingredient from trusted Himeji suppliers — tofu from Nishimoto Tofu Shop, freshly pounded mochi from Kineya, processed items from Hatoya, and seasonal seafood such as Harima-Nada octopus and conger eel at market price.

anecdotes.text: The kelp used for dashi has become increasingly precious; Ohashi-san adjusts grades and blends varieties to maintain consistent flavour as supplies tighten — a quiet testament to how seriously she takes the stock that underpins every bowl.

A personal creation on the menu — white cabbage wrapped around bacon ('白菜巻き') — sits alongside the traditional Himeji line-up, showing the proprietress's willingness to add her own touch without abandoning local identity.
The restaurant earned a Michelin Bib Gourmand listing in the 2016 Hyogo special edition, bringing wider attention to Himeji's ginger-soy oden tradition.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information on cross-contamination controls is available. The kitchen handles fish cakes and processed items that routinely contain wheat starch, and the shared oden pot means any wheat-bearing ingredient contaminates the broth for all diners.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The house ginger soy sauce is described as conventional Japanese shoyu blended with cooking sake and oden broth. Standard Japanese shoyu contains wheat. No tamari or gluten-free shoyu option is mentioned.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The shared oden pot contains bonito-based dashi throughout, as well as fish cakes, beef tendon, and seafood. There is no plant-only broth or separate cooking vessel noted.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy-handling procedures or allergen menu are mentioned on any source reviewed. Diners with celiac disease or severe allergies should contact the restaurant directly before visiting (tel: 079-284-7345).

positives.text: Dashi base is kelp and bonito — no hidden soy sauce in the broth itself. Individual pieces can in principle be chosen to avoid certain proteins (e.g., opting for daikon, konnyaku, or tofu pieces) if wheat in the dipping sauce is the only concern.

caveats: Hours and closed-days sourced from Tabelog/Gnavi listings; the restaurant operates evenings only and may close on irregular holidays — confirm before visiting. The Michelin Bib Gourmand listing is from 2016; current award status unverified. No official website found. The serai.jp article is the primary source for chef name and kitchen details; the proprietress's name (大橋仁美) could not be corroborated in a second independent source.

name_ja: 杵屋 姫路山陽百貨店
address_ja: 〒670-0912 兵庫県姫路市南町1 姫路山陽百貨店6F
geocode_note: GSI AddressSearch on 兵庫県姫路市南町1 → 134.689651,34.829166. Within target box and 491m from center point.
neighborhood: 南町 (Minamimachi)
closed_days: 山陽百貨店の休業日に準ずる
seats_text: 44席（姫路山陽百貨店6階）
cultural_comfort_note: Standard chain restaurant inside a department store; English menus are unlikely but picture menus and counter-pointing should work for basic orders.
vegan_detail: Udon and soba broth at Kineya is made with katsuobushi (bonito) and kombu dashi — not vegan. Soy sauce used in seasoning contains wheat. No dedicated vegan menu is offered. Some vegetable tempura or cold noodle side items may exist but the broth base will almost certainly contain animal-derived dashi unless staff confirm a specific item otherwise.
gf_detail: Udon noodles are made in-house from wheat flour — the core product is wheat. Soba noodles also carry cross-contamination risk from shared equipment. Soy sauce (used in broth and dipping sauces) contains wheat. The company's allergen documentation lists wheat as a primary allergen and states that cross-contamination during cooking cannot be fully prevented. No gluten-free noodle alternatives or wheat-free soy sauce are mentioned. This restaurant is not safe for celiac diners.

bio.background: Kineya is one of the flagship udon brands of Gourmet Kineya Restaurant Co. (グルメ杵屋レストラン), a large-scale Japanese noodle chain founded in 1967 (then as Ryokoku Foods). The first Kineya shop opened in 1971 inside a Daiei supermarket in Nara; the Himeji Sanyo Department Store location sits on the 6th floor of the city's main station-adjacent department store, a few steps from Sanyo-Himeji Station.
bio.specialty: Sanuki-style hand-pulled udon made daily on-site — staff demonstrate the noodle-making process in the open kitchen (実演手打うどん).
bio.philosophy: Fresh noodles prepared by hand every day using wheat flour, served with a Kansai-style katsuobushi-kombu dashi broth.
bio.origin: Osaka (corporate headquarters); Himeji branch since opening of the department store location.

anecdotes.text: The parent company, Gourmet Kineya, operates 200+ locations across Japan under multiple concepts including Kineya (udon), Soji-bo (soba), and Mugi Maru, and went public on the Osaka Securities Exchange in 1989.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Gourmet Kineya's corporate allergen documentation states that cross-contamination of allergens during cooking cannot be completely prevented across all menu items.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard soy sauce containing wheat is used in the broth and dipping sauces. All udon noodles are made in-house from wheat flour. No wheat-free soy sauce alternative is offered.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All broth is made with katsuobushi (bonito flakes) and kombu dashi — no plant-based broth option is listed. Tempura items are fried in shared oil with shrimp tempura on the menu.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The company publishes a per-item allergen PDF covering the 8 mandatory Japanese allergens (wheat, buckwheat, egg, dairy, peanut, shrimp, crab, walnut), but explicitly notes that full prevention of cross-contamination cannot be guaranteed.

positives.text: Gourmet Kineya publishes a downloadable allergen chart for all menu items on their corporate website, making it possible to check each dish before ordering.

caveats: Hours listed as 10:00-22:00 daily based on Tabelog/Hitosara; Gurunavi shows 10:00-19:30, suggesting hours may vary or differ by source — follow Sanyo Department Store's current schedule. Closed days follow the department store calendar (not a fixed weekday closure). The allergen PDF on the corporate site covers all Kineya menu items but is a scanned image and could not be fully parsed; direct contact with the restaurant for severe allergy cases is strongly advised.

address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市本町68 家老屋敷B棟
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 兵庫県姫路市本町６８番地 returned [134.687363, 34.835873]. Approximately 530m from the target center (34.8331, 134.6921), well within the 1.15km radius.
neighborhood: 本町68 家老屋敷B棟（城へ向かう大手前通り沿い）
cuisine: 揚げ物・穴子・天ぷら・丼
vegan_detail: The menu centers on anago (conger eel) donburi, fried/tempura dishes, and noodles — all seafood or meat-based. Miso soup accompanies most sets. No vegetarian or vegan options identified across any source. Dashi in soups and sauces is almost certainly fish-based (katsuobushi or niboshi). Hidden animal ingredients are expected throughout.
gf_detail: Tempura and other fried foods use standard wheat-flour batter. Noodle dishes (udon) contain wheat. Soy sauce used in the anago donburi tare and other seasonings almost certainly contains wheat. Miso soup is a staple accompaniment. No gluten-free options, dedicated fryer, or allergen accommodation information was found on any source. This is an entirely unsafe venue for celiac diners.
closed_days: 定休日の情報なし（無休の可能性あり）
seats_text: カウンター5席、テーブル5席の計10席。小さなお店。
cultural_comfort_note: 券売機で食券を購入する日本式の食堂スタイル。英語メニューや英語スタッフの情報はなく、訪日観光客向けの対応は限定的と思われる。

bio.background: 2020年5月1日、姫路城の麓・本町68の家老屋敷B棟（かつての武家屋敷エリア）にオープンした小さな食事処。城下の歴史的な建物群の一角に位置し、観光客や地元客が城見物の合間に立ち寄るランチスポットとして機能している。
bio.specialty: 姫路の名物・穴子（アナゴ）を使った穴子丼。瀬戸内海産の穴子を蒸し焼きにし、刻み海苔と生姜をのせた一品で、食べログのレビュアーが「人力車のお兄さんに勧められた」と書くほど地元で口コミが広まっている。
bio.origin: 姫路市本町68 家老屋敷B棟

anecdotes.text: 食べログのレビュアーは「人力車のお兄さんに勧められた」と書き、穴子の身が「プリプリ」でリーズナブルと高評価。券売機で食券を購入する昔ながらの大衆食堂スタイルが、観光地の中に素朴な地元感を残している。

gf_cross_contamination.text: 揚げ物（天ぷら・フライ）を主力とする店で、小麦粉の衣を使用する調理が常時行われている。フライヤーの共用状況は不明だが、グルテン汚染のリスクは極めて高い。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: 穴子丼のたれや各種料理には通常の醤油（小麦含有）が使用されていると推定される。グルテンフリー醤油の使用を示す情報は皆無。

vegan_cross_contact.text: 魚介（穴子・白魚）を中心とするメニュー構成で、動物性食材を使わない料理の選択肢はない。みそ汁のだしも動物性と推定される。

caveats: No official website found. Closed days unconfirmed — multiple sources list no regular closed day but this should be verified by phone before visiting. Hours (11:00-16:00 daily) consistent across all sources but may vary seasonally. The ticket-machine ordering system and small size (10 seats) mean waits are possible during peak castle-visit hours. No allergen or dietary accommodation information exists in any public source — celiac and vegan diners should avoid.

cuisine: カフェ・パン・ベーグル
address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市本町68-7
geocode_note: GSI geocode returned lat 34.835873, lng 134.687363 for 兵庫県姫路市本町68-7. Approx 0.64km from target center (34.8331, 134.6921), within 1.15km radius.
neighborhood: 本町 / 姫路城南
seats_text: カウンター・テーブル席あり、屋外テラスあり（ペット同伴可）
vegan_detail: No vegan options identified. The menu is built around bread, butter, and meat-based items: all-you-can-eat bread from the sister bakery (includes tuna sandwiches, butter/jam spreads), NY-style beef hamburgers, chicken bánh mì, and American-style sweets. No plant-based or dairy-free alternatives mentioned on any source reviewed.
gf_detail: This is a bread-and-bakery café whose signature attraction is an all-you-can-eat bread spread featuring 20+ wheat-based items from sister bakery BAKERY 燈 LAMP. Burgers, bagels, bánh mì, donuts, and cinnamon rolls all contain gluten. No gluten-free alternatives, dedicated fryer, or allergen management information was found on any source. Not appropriate for celiac diners.

bio.background: Cafe SOL opened on February 23, 2019 as the third concept location of popular Himeji bakery BAKERY 燈 LAMP (Bakery Lamp). The name SOL is Spanish for 'sun,' reflecting the owner's intention to create a bright, relaxed space. The café occupies a wood-framed building with high ceilings, large windows, and a courtyard terrace — an unusual retreat tucked behind the Honmachi shopping arcade, a five-minute walk from Himeji Castle.
bio.specialty: パン食べ放題モーニング（20種以上のパンが食べ放題）とNYスタイルのハンバーガー
bio.philosophy: Bright and comfortable; an everyday escape for castle-district locals and visitors alike, blending baked goods from the sister bakery with international café fare.

anecdotes.text: The all-you-can-eat bread morning service — over 20 varieties baked fresh at BAKERY 燈 LAMP and offered in mini sizes — became the café's breakout attraction, drawing weekend queues despite its tucked-away location.
Cafe SOL was billed as 'the third location of the popular Bakery Lamp chain,' and opened in 2019 as a full sit-down café experience built around bread culture.

gf_cross_contamination.text: The entire menu is wheat-centric — bread, pastries, burgers, and sandwiches are the core offering. No allergen labelling or cross-contamination management information was found on any source.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Bread spread includes butter and egg-based items; American-style sweets contain dairy. No dairy-free or vegan cross-contact protocols mentioned.

cultural_comfort_note: One early review noted English signage and a relaxed tourist-friendly atmosphere near Himeji Castle, but the café operates entirely in Japanese and no dedicated English menu or English-speaking staff is confirmed.
caveats: Hours vary and the café advises checking Instagram (@_cafe_sol_) before visiting — weekend-only operation means holidays may add or shift open days. The Gnavi listing notes a 14-minute walk from Sanyo Himeji Station, longer than the Tabelog-listed 7 minutes; actual walk time depends on route. No official standalone website found; Square mobile ordering site (cafe-sol-mobile.square.site) was not fully accessible.

cuisine: Japanese Kaiseki / Omakase Sushi / Setouchi Seasonal
address_ja: 兵庫県姫路市魚町129 花むらビル1F
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI AddressSearch for 兵庫県姫路市魚町129; result within target box (approx. 285m from target center 34.8331,134.6921). Confirmed in target area.
neighborhood: 魚町・山陽姫路駅近
seats_text: 15 seats total: 11 counter seats facing the open kitchen, plus one private room seating 4
cultural_comfort_note: Counter-only omakase format with a young Japanese chef working in an open kitchen; no English menu or signage noted across any sources, though online reservations via Tabelog/HotPepper are accessible to non-Japanese speakers.
